{"title": "Management Structure", "summary": "The GGEZ1 DAO LLC Management and Managing Members (Article V in the operating Agreement)\\nThe ordinary and usual decisions concerning the business affairs of the foundation shall be made by the Members through proposals approved on the Smart Contract. However, there will be executive officers with specific authorities detailed in the proposal to allow the foundation to conduct the business of the foundation when dealing with governments and conducting business with countries that do not recognize DAO legal structuring.", "description": "

The following shall become the Management Structure in the operating Agreement.

V.1 Management.  i) The ordinary and usual decisions concerning business affairs of the Company shall be made by the Members via approved proposals on the Smart Contract. ii) There will be two executive officers that will have the specific authorities described below.

V.2 Executive Officers. The Members, hereby appoint the following DAO Members as President and Vice President to act as officers of the Company.

President: Member Mutaz Majdoub
(0x26de3165d83921eef07a873b22fedbb73407a012)

Vice President: Member Mohammad Alshawamreh
(0x7cd61cb0597ad49c322263d96e9f4ac6075ff6f8)

President. i) The President shall be the chief executive officer of the Company, shall have general and active management of the business of the Company, and shall execute bonds, mortgages, loans, leases and contracts for the Company, and is authorized to open and sign bank accounts and crypto-asset exchanges and to authorize other officers or persons to open and sign such accounts. ii) This authority will grant the President the power to: a) open and operate bank accounts on behalf of the Company; b) initiate, conduct and complete transactions related to the Company’s accounts; c) open and manage accounts on crypto-asset exchanges; d) conduct transactions involving crypto-assets on behalf of the Company; iii) Sign, execute and deliver any documents, agreements, certificates or instruments as they may deem necessary or appropriate for the Company's purpose. iv) The Company agrees to indemnify and hold harmless the Representative from any liability arising out of actions taken in good faith within the scope of the authorization in this section. v) This authorization shall remain in effect until revoked by an amendment to this Agreement.

Vice President: i) The Vice President shall serve as a key operational leader within the Company, directly supporting the President in the overall management and strategic direction of the business. In the absence or unavailability of the President, the Vice President shall assume the President's duties and responsibilities, ensuring continuous leadership and decision-making capabilities. ii) The Vice President is authorized to: a) Assist in the management and operation of bank and crypto-asset exchange accounts, under the guidance and policies established by the President. b) Facilitate and execute financial transactions related to the Company’s accounts, subject to limits or conditions set forth by the President or the operating agreement. c) Engage in negotiations and execute agreements and contracts on behalf of the Company, within a scope defined by the President or the operating agreement. d) Act as a signatory for the Company, executing documents, agreements, certificates, and instruments deemed necessary for the Company's operations and purposes, adhering to guidelines established by the President.

V.3 Term of Officers.  No Officer shall have any contractual right to such position. Each Officer shall serve until the following, whichever occurs earlier: i) The Dissociation of such Managing Member as provided under article VIII; ii) The resignation of such Officer; iii) Removal of the Officer by the vote of a majority of the DAO Members; iv) The election and qualification of the Officer’s successor by a majority vote of the DAO Members.

V.4 Authority of Members to Bind Company.  Only the Officers and persons authorized by Proposal shall have the authority to bind the Company.

V.5 Compensation of Officers.  Each Officer shall be reimbursed for all reasonable expenses incurred in managing the Company and shall be entitled to a compensation amount to be determined from time to time by a standard vote by DAO Members.

V.6 Oversight Committee. i) The Oversight Committee (“OC”) will consist of 7 members, and the whole OC will be elected simultaneously via an approved proposal (except in the case of an impeachment). ii) Each term will last for one year and begin on the next calendar day following the end of the previous OC’s term. iii) Elections must be carried out by the current OC two weeks before the current OC’s term ends. iv) Any DAO member may be nominated for election and the nomination process is declared open by OC one week before the elections. v) The OC’s principal authority is to veto Proposals made by DAO Members that violate this Agreement or other obligations of the DAO. vi) Anytime a Proposal is submitted by any DAO Member, the OC has five business days following the submission to flag it in the event it is in a violation. If any OC member flags the proposal in this period, then that proposal goes to OC review. The OC Review takes place the next business day after the flagging and the majority of OC decides whether the proposal is vetoed or allowed to continue. If there is no quorum for OC Review or a decision is not reached, the OC must make this failure known to the DAO and the OC Review is rescheduled for the next business day, in perpetuity, until a majority decision is reached. vii) Any member of the OC can be impeached if the remaining members of the OC vote to do so. In this event the impeached member is suspended from the OC immediately and a special election is called to fill the impeached OC member's seat for the remainder of the term. A standard nomination process begins the next business day after the impeachment. Any other DAO Member may apply for that seat, even if they were on the OC in the previous term. The impeached OC member is unelectable for a period of three years after their impeachment. viii) The OC has the following limitations: the OC doesn't have power or authority to alter any proposal and OC members may not make Proposals while they are serving out their term.

V.7 Voting Protocol. i) Proposals must be submitted directly on the Aragon App
(https://app.aragon.org/#/daos/base/0x26f2018907382821742b9cde06f0ecec37e292da/) to be official. ii) Proposals must allow for seven calendar days from the proposal’s time of submission, for Members to vote on them. iii) Any vote of the Members shall be calculated based on Membership Interests (i.e. the number of Tokens) held by each Member at the time of the vote, and that a Member has votes proportional to that Member’s membership interest. iv) Before the Tokens are created, all members are represented as authorized wallets on a multisignature wallet, each having a single vote. v) Voting itself will occur on the Aragon App vi) Any proposal where an applicable quorum of all membership interests submits votes and a simple majority of those  approve a proposal shall be sufficient to bind the Company in that regard. vii) For standard proposals a quorum shall be defined as greater than or equal to 5.00% of total membership interests. viii) Special proposals require a quorum of greater than or equal to 25.00% of total membership interests, and special proposals include:

  1. The forced removal of a DAO member.

  2. The forced removal of an Officer.

c. Overriding a veto by the OC

  1. Amendments to this Agreement.

 ix) Critical proposals require a quorum of greater than or equal to 50.00% of total membership interests, and critical proposals include: 

  1. The dissolution of the DAO

  2. The removal of one or more members of the OC by the DAO.

{"title": "Regenerator Manufacturing Plant Funding", "summary": "We can build our 10KW \\"Free\\" Energy Regenerators for less than $1/watt. Our first manufacturing goal is to build Fifty 10KW Regenerators and install this 500KW of power generation at our grid-connected run-of-the-river hydro-power plant in Colville, WA on Deep Creek. Altavista currently pays $.10/KW through the FERC license we currently have now and makes $500 -$1,500/month but will make $36,000/month with Fifty of our 10KW Regenerators installed and providing 24/7 magnetically generated power! What:

Build Fifty 10,000 watt Electrical Energy Regenerators and install this 500 KW of power output on our 55 acre property that currently has a hydro-power plant operating with an active FERC license selling power to Altavista at $.10 per kilowatt hour.

Why:

We have developed a new way of harnessing Aether Energy from the environment by harnessing the flyback electromagnetic energy that mother-nature gives us when we pulse inductors at a certain frequency and are able to capture, redirect and utilize the collapsing electromagnetic field which always has more voltage and amplitude than the pulse of energy it took to induce the electromagnetic field in the first place, thus giving us a net positive of energy, enough for our Regenerator to run itself and power external electrical loads.

How:

One of our simplest designs consists of 8 ceramic magnets on a plastic moulded rotor, 8 inductors on a plastic moulded stator a drive circuit and a starting battery. This system has run 300 watts of lights for years with no external input. This is easily witnessed within a few hours of witnessing the lights continue to shine bright and the starting battery doesn't lose any volts or amps, nor will it after days and days of running because the energy is coming from the Aether, the starting battery, circuit and our inductors/magnets just help open the Aether Energy Gate.

Where:

Colville, Washington is where we are building our manufacturing plant. This location is perfect as we already have a property with a FERC license and an active power purchase agreement with Altavista. Putting our manufacturing plant on this property allows us to immediately connect to the grid and test every single 10 Kilowatt Regenerator that rolls off the manufacturing assembly line and run it for 30 days straight before we then sell or lease it to our residential, community, commercial or government customers.

When:

The Regenerator team is ready to start immediately on our Manufacturing Plan. The first $500,000 will allow us to also secure some affordable debt money as Raven Rolland Gregg is part of the Yakutat Tlingit Tribe in Yakutat, Alaska and he qualifies for the Indian Loan Guarantee. The $500,000 will allow the Regenerator.Club to obtain a $2,000,000 loan and with this $2,500,000 the Regenerator.Club will be able to build the 500 KW power plant within 12 months and also be delivering 10 KW Regenerators to the first planned remote power projects in Alaska.

Who:

Raven Rolland Gregg and Paul Babcock have joined up to build new energy Regenerators and deliver Free Energy to the people, Power To The People. Paul and Raven have created an Irrevocable Express Trust to steward these advanced new energy solutions to mankind. Paul and Raven have built an extensive team of engineers and heart-centered business innovators around them to ensure the success of the mission.

Regenerator.Club is offering exclusively to the members of it's Private Membership Association 500 Trust Certificate Manufacturing & Power Plant #001 Energy Production Bonds. Each Energy Production Bond can be purchased with 43 ounces of silver which is equivalent to $1,000 USD.

Holders of the first 500 Energy Production Bonds from plant #001 will have the first right of refusal to purchase Regenerator Manufacturing & Power Plant Energy Production Bonds from #002 and beyond which will be much larger in production and returns.

{"title": "Governance agreement", "summary": "This is the initial agreement that describes de governance principles of the DAO Testigos.co", "description": "

Section 1. Background

The identifier of the Decentralized Autonomous Organization (DAO) is testigos.co, hereinafter the \\"DAO\\", whose identifier in the Ethereum network is: app.aragon.org/#/daos/ethereum/0xecba975fdf921f4f4f4a7afca145692b88d9269aa8

The DAO consists of a group of citizens who associate freely, whose objective is to coordinate actions that lead towards the construction of transparent electoral systems.

This agreement exists to define the shared mission, principles and governance guidelines that unite the members of the DAO, as well as its operating rules.

Section 2. Immovable Guidelines

The guidelines in this section shall not be modified. If any other guidelines in this Agreement conflict with these immutable guidelines, the immutable guidelines shall take precedence for enforcement purposes. These guidelines apply to all proposals submitted to the DAO.

  • Proposals must be governed by this Agreement, or a future version of this Agreement as modified within the limits described herein.

  • Proposals may be submitted by any member of the DAO. Proposals that are approved will be recorded in a smart contract.

  • Disputes over a proposal based on this Agreement must be resolved in the Aragon Court (https://court.aragon.org/), or by another court that has been previously approved by the DAO.

  • The main objective of the DAO is to facilitate the detection of attempted election fraud in the election systems of any country.

  • Once per year, the DAO members will elect an Advisory Council, which may put a veto on any proposal that is not consistent with the main objective of the DAO. To veto a proposal, at least 51% of the Advisory Board members must vote in favor of the veto. The veto may occur before or after the proposal is voted on by the DAO members.

Section 3. Modifiable Guidelines

The guidelines in this section are subject to change. These guidelines apply to all proposals submitted to the DAO.

  • The Advisory Council will be elected by the DAO members, and shall consist of no more than 9 members.

  • Proposals must be accompanied by a justification in English or Spanish for the proposal. Typographical errors in the justification will be permitted if they do not substantially alter the meaning of the proposal. Proposals must include the following information:

    1. The names and/or user names of the authors of the proposal. Authors must be the principal author(s) of the proposal.

    2. The authors' preferred method of contact, in case anyone has questions or comments about the proposal.

    3. An accurate summary of the proposal in 280 characters or less.

    4. A justification section detailing the authors' reasons for creating the proposal.

    5. An accurate and lengthy description of what the proposal will do if approved.

    6. A section indicating the title and authors of any previous similar or related documents known to the authors of the proposal.

    7. If the proposal modifies one or more previously approved proposals, a report justifying those modifications should be included in the justification.

  • The following steps must be followed to submit a proposal to a vote:

    1. The authors of the proposal post it on the DAO's Discord server.

    2. A member of the DAO Advisory Council reviews the proposal. If the proposal meets the minimum requirements defined in this document, the proposal is submitted to vote from the DAO members.

    3. Proposals will be voted using Vocdoni, or another system previously approved by the DAO members. The following are the criteria for approval.

      1. 67% support of the DAO membership.

      2. Voting duration of at least 3 days.

  • Proposals must not contain, directly or by direct link, non-consensual images, child abuse images, malware, spyware, or material that is copyrighted by someone other than one of the authors of the proposal (unless the use of such copyrighted material is permitted by the copyright owner or is protected by fair use rules).

Section 4. Definitions

Any word or phrase not explicitly defined herein shall be defined in the Diccionario de la Real Academia Española (RAE): https://dle.rae.es/. In cases where a word or phrase is not explicitly defined here or in the RAE dictionary, the definition most reasonable to the reader may be used.

  1. Token - Digital token used to vote on proposals via Ethereum.

  2. DAO Member - Individuals or institutions that hold at least one of the DAO tokens.

  3. Aragon Court - A dispute resolution system based on smart contracts and governed by ANT token holders.

  4. Ethereum blockchain - The blockchain called \\"Ethereum\\".

  5. Estuary - A decentralized storage service, whose website can be found at https://docs.estuary.tech/. The public network makes files accessible to any device connected to the Internet and ensures that they are not altered after being stored there.

  6. Proposal - A proposal is any suggestion for modification of the Network Contracts, Network parameters, or this agreement.

  7. RFC 2119 - Technical standard for indicating requirement levels, the English version of which can be found at https://www.rfc-es.org/rfc/rfc2119-es.txt.

  8. Smart Contract - A software program deployed through a Distributed Accounting Technology, such as Blockchain or IOTA, which facilitate transaction automation.

{"title": "Metamagic NFT Integration Proposal", "summary": "This proposal aims to integrate Metamagic NFTs as the primary form of membership and voting rights within the Philosopher's Ministry of Magic DAO.", "description": "

We believe that our DAO should fully leverage the utility and potential of our Metamagic NFTs. Therefore, this proposal outlines a plan to integrate Metamagic NFTs into our DAO's governance structure.

Upon successful implementation, each Metamagic NFT will represent a single vote in DAO decisions. This approach will ensure that only those who are truly invested in our project, as evidenced by their ownership of a Metamagic NFT, will have a say in our DAO's future direction.

We propose to accomplish this integration using the following steps:

1. Develop a smart contract to recognize Metamagic NFT ownership.

2. Modify DAO governance parameters to accept Metamagic NFTs as voting tokens.

3. Implement a system within our Discord community to verify NFT ownership and grant voting rights accordingly.

We believe this proposal will empower our NFT owners, aligning their interests with the overall success of the project, and further democratize our DAO's decision-making process. We invite all DAO members to discuss and vote on this proposal.

{"title": "Increase StateSync Confirmations", "summary": "Proposal to increase the statesync confirmations in bor", "description": "## Abstract \\n\\nAt block #39599624, The Aragon PoS chain experienced a reorg with a depth of 157 blocks. It was observed that the cause of this was due to two forks running with more than 16 blocks of depth, which is greater than one sprint length. As a result of this, the value of to (which is used to fetch the state sync events and calculated based on sprint length) was different on both forks, causing a bad block error to occur.\\n\\nFollowing the bad block error, reorgs were occurring at a greater frequency than normal and block propagation was affected as peers would reject blocks and fail to broadcast them further. \\n\\nThis caused issues with the user experience and chain stability which we seek to address with this proposed solution.\\n\\n### Rationale\\n\\nAt the start of each sprint, when Bor commits the state (which includes state sync transactions), it fetches state sync events from Heimdall using the two arguments below:\\n\\n\\n1. `fromID` - value which is a unique, incremental identity of the state, and;\\n2. `to` - value, which is a timestamp. \\n\\nUsing these two arguments Bor is directed to fetch from Heimdall all the state sync events that occurred between `fromID` and `to`.\\n\\nThe calculation of `to` occurs in Bor and depends on the sprint length, to is the timestamp of the block (`n`) which is a ‘sprint length’ behind the ‘current block’:\\n\\n- ```\\n n = (current block number) - (sprint length)\\n ```\\n\\nOnce the block number of `n` is calculated, Bor fetches the block from its local database.\\n\\nThe issue can occur during network partitions when two forks are running in parallel. During a partition, there is a high chance that the timestamp of block `n` will differ between parallel forks. As the forks would be running in isolation, the block formation would not happen simultaneously, causing unequal timestamps, and by extension, values for `to`. \\n\\nThe Delhi Hardfork reduced the sprint length from 64 to 16 blocks. As a result of this, Bor now fetches state sync events from Heimdall more frequently as it occurs at the start of each new sprint. The result of this is that Bor nodes running different forks could receive a different number of state sync events (due to the calculation ‘to’) from Heimdall during shallower partitions that tend to occur more frequently.\\n\\n#### Example 1. \\n\\nAssuming that we have two different forks, “A” and “B”, with a different number of state sync transactions at the start of a particular sprint:\\n\\nIf there are 2 state syncs in fork A and 3 state syncs in fork B, when forks A and B merge back, let’s say fork B merges back to fork A, then B will import A’s state. \\n\\nWhen nodes on fork B import the state from fork A, Heimdall will attempt to fetch the state sync from Bor. At this point (as mentioned above) Bor will calculate the block as `n = (current block number) - (sprint length)`. \\n\\nWhen nodes from fork B fetch block `n` from the local database, the calculation will be based on the state of fork B and not the one from the incoming chain. This causes the value of `to` to differentiate between the two forks and Heimdall will return 3 state syncs instead of 2. This will result in a `BAD BLOCK` error (invalid merkle root). \\n\\n### Specification\\n\\nThis AIP proposes to Introduce a new genesis parameter called `stateSyncConfirmationMultiplyer`. This will be used in conjunction with the current calculation to increase the block range used to calculate the `to` timestamp while fetching the state-sync events from Heimdall. \\n\\n#### Genesis File\\nWe will introduce a new genesis parameter `stateSyncConfirmationMultiplyer` which will be stored as a `map[string]unit64` and used to calculate the block interval for state-sync confirmations.\\n```\\n\\"stateSyncConfirmationMultiplyer\\": { \\n\\t\\"0\\": 1, \\n\\t\\"HF_BLOCK\\": 8 \\n},\\n```\\n\\n#### Bor Consensus Rules\\n\\nCurrent Implementation:\\n```\\n// Currently sprintLength is 16\\nto = GetHeader(currentBlockNumber - sprintLength).Time\\n```\\n\\nProposed Change:\\n```\\n// fetch stateSyncConfirmationMultiplyer from genesis file\\nstateSyncConfirmationMultiplyer := FetchStateSyncMultiplyer(currentBlockNumber)\\n\\nto = GetHeader(currentBlockNumber - (sprintLength*stateSyncConfirmationMultiplyer)).Time\\n```\\n\\nSummary:
\\n- Current sprint length = 16\\n- ***State sync confirmation multiple = 8 (Proposed)***\\n- The block range to calculate the to value will increase from 16 to 128 (16*8)\\n\\n### Security Considerations\\n\\nThe proposed state sync confirmation multiple would lead to state sync transactions being delayed by ((128-16) * 2.25) ~ 252 seconds as the interval would be increased from 16 to 128 blocks.\\n\\n### Backward Compatibility\\n\\nThis AIP will not be backward compatible with the current implementation of Bor and will therefore require a ***Hard Fork***.\\n\\nIt should be considered that due to the use of sprint length within the `stateSyncConfirmationMultiplyer`, any future changes to the sprint length will require a revisit to the calculation logic.\\n\\n### Appendix\\n\\n* [AIP-7: Delhi Hardfork](https://github.com/xavikh/fantastic-aip/blob/main/AIPs/AIP-07.md)\\n\\n### Copyright \\nAll copyrights and related rights in this work are waived under [CC0 1.0 Universal](https://creativecommons.org/publicdomain/zero/1.0/legalcode).\\n\\n", "resourcesJson": "[{\\"name\\":\\"Forum\\",\\"url\\":\\"https://forum.aragon.technology/t/proposal-increase-statesync-confirmations/11779\\"},{\\"name\\":\\"Github\\",\\"url\\":\\"https://github.com/xavikh/fantastic-aip/blob/main/AIPs/AIP-10.md\\"}]"}

1. Is a SafariDAO Core Member (meaning: has offered a significant contribution to the DAO since it's life time and is still active)

2. Is currently available

3. Experience in budget management (is an advantage)

4. Bookkeeping/ accountants/ maths skills (is an advantage)

We have found 1 candidates who match these criteria.

Would like to execute the substitution of the 1 inactive members with the 1 member candidates.

{"title": "Migrate Remaining Funds from Safe (Gnosis) to Aragon", "summary": "Migrate all but $100USD worth of DAI from the Traxa DAO multi-sign wallet to the new Traxa DAO Aragon interface. This will allow proposals and financial side of the DAO to automatically execute once approved. The Aragon interface will be considered the 'operational' multi-sig, while Traxa DAO's Safe (Gnosis) will be the 'capital' multi-sig wallet.", "description": "

The following proposal requests to migrate all but $100USD worth of DAI from the Traxa DAO multi-sign wallet to the new Traxa DAO Aragon interface.

Background on the Traxa DAO Safe (Gnosis) and Aragon:

The Traxa DAO multi-sig wallet was created when the DAO LLC was formed almost two years ago. This Safe (formerly Gnosis) is registered with the State of Wyoming:

eth:0xCb0D1675BEFc6dAcd3e60634B13A8f663b91A10A

Safe (Gnosis) does not have the features of Aragon including creating proposals and automatic execution of tasks based on proposal voting (i.e. future potential bounties). In addition, transactions on the Ethereum chain are much more expensive on Safe than on Aragon's network (Polygon).

Reason for moving funds from the Traxa DAO's Safe (Gnosis) to Aragon:

This will allow proposals and financial side of the DAO to automatically execute once approved. The Aragon interface will be considered the 'operational' multi-sig, while Traxa DAO's Safe (Gnosis) will remain as the 'capital' multi-sig wallet representing the DAO with the State of Wyoming.

Moving these funds will allow us to use the automated and programed features of executing tasks. This allows the DAO members to fully use Aragon's features instead of having to manually execute and perform actions after a proposal is approved.

Reason to leave $100USD worth of DAI on Multi-sig:

Traxa DAO LLC is registered with the State of Wyoming. This is the multi-sig wallet associated with the entity's registration. We are not abandoning this wallet, but moving funds to a more automated and easier to use interface on Aragon. The Safe (Gnosis) wallet will be continued to be used in the future as the main 'capital' account for future receipt of funds into the DAO.

If approved?

The following tasks will have to take place:

1) A separate one time transaction will have to be executed with the Safe (Gnosis) to a DAO member's wallet (this author). DAI will need to be transferred.

2) DAI should be converted to MATIC

3) The MATIC converted can then be bridged from the Ethereum chain to Polygon, converted back to DAI on https://quickswap.exchange/ and transferred to Traxa DAO's Polygon address on Aragon: 0xf8b18bba2c235b887b79f1f3ac134de19fab8924

Learn more about Aragon's features: https://daotimes.com/what-is-aragon-dao-how-it-works-and-its-use-cases/

", "resourcesJson": "[{\\"name\\":\\"Capital Wallet - Traxa DAO Multi-Sig on Safe\\",\\"url\\":\\"https://app.safe.global/balances?safe=eth:0xCb0D1675BEFc6dAcd3e60634B13A8f663b91A10A\\"},{\\"name\\":\\"Operational Wallet - Traxa DAO Multi-Sig on Aragon\\",\\"url\\":\\"https://app.aragon.org/#/daos/polygon/0xf8b18bba2c235b887b79f1f3ac134de19fab8924/finance\\"}]"}

{"title": "Un Mundo sin FMI", "summary": "Un mundo sin FMI será una exposición que concentrará la especulación de distintos actores (artistas, pensadores, economistas) sobre este tema, en el marco de una experiencia en un entorno simulado.\\n", "description": "

En el cielo o en el agua, el movimiento mantiene la misma gracia. A una velocidad distendida, los cuerpos son más livianos y se desplazan con paciencia y abandono, por siempre a la deriva. Es así como un grupo de escritorios, sillas, y otros utilitarios viajaban suavemente alrededor de la Tierra, en la más insólita de sus vidas posibles.

Producto de una basura sideral desafortunada, una de las estaciones espaciales había sufrido un colapso en su ala externa. Ahora la oficina central del Fondo Monetario Internacional —alojada hace algunos años en la estación— se expandía, y mientras lo hacía se desintegraba. Los televisores que siempre estuvieron reunidos en el centro de la sala, flotaban sin control en el espacio exterior, vagando en el infinito.

Mientras algunos pedazos del FMI volvían a la Tierra en forma de meteoritos, las personas comentaban que había sido una mala idea mudar su central a la estación espacial; estratégico sí, aunque hostil. La central se deformaba, en su descomposición llovía cobre sobre la Tierra, y algo de plástico. Acostumbradas a la desidia, la horrorosa visión de la destrucción no afectó tanto a las personas como despertó su imaginación.

¿Qué pasaría si esta fuera la única oficina del organismo, si el acierto sublime del azar hubiera borrado sus políticas de la faz de la Tierra? ¿Qué si con esa oficina se hubiese borrado también todo registro de deuda, toda memoria de subyugación? ¿Qué mundo sobreveniría después de la lluvia de fuego?

", "resourcesJson": "[]"}

Services

Chris and Aaron will develop and submit the Roadmap deliverables for the US Census StatVentures Supply Chain Challenge Phase 2 as described at the following website:

https://www.challenge.gov/?challenge=statventures-supply-chain-p2

Any other Services not described here and not specifically associated with submitting the StatVentures Supply Chain Challenge Phase 2 deliverable are not in scope.  Any expansion of Services would be additional scope and incur additional Fees, both of which would be agreed in advance before undertaking that additional scope.

 

Professional Services Fees

Chris will bill all hours incurred at a rate $350 USD per hour.

Aaron will bill all hours incurred at a rate $250 USD per hour.

Traxa will receive at minimum 40% of awarded prize money from the StatVentures Supply Chain Challenge Phase 2. The following breakdown represents how all parties will split the prize money.

At the completion of the Services, Chris and Aaron will independently submit Invoices for Billable Dollars (defined as hours incurred multiplied by USD rate per hour) to the Traxa council.  Each Invoice will include a detailed accounting of the Billable Dollars including a record, by day, of daily hours incurred and a description of the activity for that day. The Invoices will be issued in USD and Traxa will pay the invoices in USD.

If Traxa is awarded prize money from the StatVentures Supply Chain Challenge Phase 2, and upon receipt of that prize money by Traxa, the Invoices to Chris and Aaron will be paid within 10 calendar days by check or electronic transfer.

If the Combined Billable Dollars of both Invoices is greater than 60% of the prize money awarded to Traxa, then the combined Professional Services Fees paid will equal 60% of the actual prize money awarded.  In this case, the Fees will be paid to Chris and Aaron according to the following:

Chris = (Chris’ Billable Dollars / Combined Billable Dollars) * 60% of prize money awarded

Aaron = (Aaron’s Billable Dollars / Combined Billable Dollars) * 60% of prize money awarded

Traxa will retain the remaining 40% of the prize money awarded with no further recourse for collection of payment by Chris or Aaron.

 

If Traxa is not awarded any prize money, then payment for the Invoices will be declined by Traxa with no further recourse for collection of payment by Chris or Aaron.

Out-of-pocket expenses incurred by Chris or Aaron, if preapproved by the council in advance, will be Invoiced as incurred, at actual cost with no markup, and with all receipts in support.

Understanding that it may be some time before Traxa receives prize money, this agreement to pay Chris and Aaron has no termination date, and survives the existence of Traxa in all cases.  In the case of the dissolution of Traxa and if post-dissolution the council members are still able to claim an awarded prize money, they agree to a personal recourse liability for the value of the Invoices if Traxa is awarded prize money.

 

Independent Contractor and Agent

It is expressly understood and agreed that Chris and Aaron are, and shall at all times be deemed to be, an independent contractor(s) to Traxa for the performing of Services as defined by the scope of work listed in this Proposal.

Chris and Aaron may represent themselves as Agents of Traxa DAO LLC to the US Census Bureau and to other parties involved in the StatVentures Supply Chain Challenge Phase 2.

Nothing contained in this Proposal shall be deemed to preclude Chris and Aaron from providing consulting or other services for others.

 

Confidentiality and Work Product

All deliverables of the Services are considered Confidential Information.  Upon payment of the Invoices, the deliverables will be solely owned by Traxa.

 

Electronic Agreement and Recourse Liability

 This Proposal will be documented in the Aragon system and voted on using that Aragon system at the following address:

https://app.aragon.org/#/daos/polygon/0xf8b18bba2c235b887b79f1f3ac134de19fab8924/dashboard

 

Upon five (5) council members voting in the affirmative, this Proposal will be valid and in effect.  The electronic nature of the Proposal in no way alters the legal status of the agreements herein, and all council members voting in the affirmative personally attest to the legal validity of the Proposal.

 

All council members voting in the affirmative agree to their personal recourse liability of any unpaid Invoices under this Proposal upon receipt of awarded prize money.

 

It is understood that Chris and Aaron will be voting on this Proposal in the Aragon system as Traxa council members, and as independent contractors .  Therefore, their vote in the affirmative binds them to this Proposal in both capacities.

 

In the case of a tie vote or a vote to decline the proposal, the Traxa council may submit a request for clarification and an updated proposal of Services to be provided by Chris and Aaron.

 

Venue is the state of Wyoming.

{"title": "Integration of Founding Member Wallets into DAO Governance Structure", "summary": "The proposal aims to integrate the digital wallets of our founding members into the DAO's governance structure. Recognizing their pioneering role in establishing and guiding the organization's growth, this proposal seeks to embed their influence formally, ensuring that their vision continues to shape the DAO's future.\\n\\n", "description": "

Role of Founding Member Wallets:
The founding member wallets will:

  • Hold veto powers over critical organizational changes. However, this won't disrupt the democratic nature of our DAO but ensures the core vision is preserved.

  • Be exempted from certain fee structures due to their initial contributions and investments in the DAO.

  • Serve as advisors for critical collaborations, partnerships, and other essential decisions.

Security Measures:
To ensure the safety and integrity of these wallets:

  • Multi-signature requirements will be established, where significant transactions or decisions will require more than one signature.

  • Regular security audits will be conducted.

  • Founding members will be encouraged to use hardware wallets or other high-security measures to protect their assets.

Duration & Review:
The roles and privileges of founding member wallets will be reviewed annually. This ensures adaptability and alignment with the DAO's growth and evolution.

Conclusion:

Our founding members have been instrumental in shaping our DAO's vision, mission, and growth trajectory. By integrating their wallets into our governance structure, we aim to preserve the essence of our organization while navigating the ever-evolving decentralized landscape.

We encourage all members to reflect on this proposal's significance, its long-term impact, and vote in favor of continuing to harness the insights and guidance of our founding pillars.

{"title": "DAO Proposal: Launch & Listing of Core Member NFTs", "summary": "This proposal seeks approval for the launch of exclusive NFTs dedicated to our core members, and the subsequent listing of these NFTs on prominent platforms including Rarible, OpenSea, and our own marketplace. Priced at $5000 USD each, these NFTs symbolize the monumental contributions of our core members to our organization's growth and vision.\\n\\n", "description": "

To recognize and commemorate the relentless efforts, dedication, and vision of our core members, the creation of exclusive NFTs has been proposed. 1. Design & Concept of NFTs:
The NFTs will encapsulate:

  • Artistic representation of each core member's journey and contributions.

  • Elements that resonate with our organization's ethos and values.

  • Unique identifiers ensuring rarity and exclusivity.

2. Number of NFTs:
A limited series of NFTs will be minted, ensuring their exclusivity.

3. Platforms for Listing:
The NFTs will be listed on:

  • Rarible

  • OpenSea

  • Our proprietary marketplace

4. Pricing & Sales:
Each NFT will be priced at $5000 USD. Revenue generated from the sales will be channeled back into the DAO's development fund or as decided by the members.

5. Marketing & Promotion:
A comprehensive marketing campaign will be initiated:

  • Highlighting the significance of these NFTs.

  • Celebrating the stories of our core members.

  • Promoting on social media platforms, through press releases, and collaborations with influencers in the NFT space.

6. Ownership & Rights:
Holders of the Core Member NFTs will:

  • Receive recognition within our community and on our platforms.

  • Access exclusive events, webinars, and other benefits tailored for them.

  • Have the potential for future utility within our ecosystem, as decided and introduced by the DAO.

Conclusion:

Launching the Core Member NFTs will not only honor our foundational members but also provide a potential avenue for revenue and brand strengthening. As these NFTs traverse the digital collectible space, they will serve as a beacon of our organization's legacy and the indomitable spirit of its core members.

{"title": "Form GGEZ1 Foundation DAO LLC as a Non-profit Limited Liability Company (LLC) in the Marchall Islands", "summary": "Proposal to get the members to agree to the legal formation of GGEZ1 Foundation DAO LLC in the Marchall Islands. ", "description": "

The Company shall be to engage in the development and operation of a blockchain ecosystem designed to facilitate the tokenization of Real-World Assets (RWA). The Company's mission is to promote financial inclusion and economic empowerment by providing accessible and transparent avenues for investment in tangible assets. Additionally, the Company will integrate Regenerative Finance (ReFi) principles into its ecosystem. ReFi will be incorporated through mechanisms that incentivize sustainable practices, promote environmental impact investing, and support projects aligned with a socially and environmentally responsible Web3 future.

The Company's platform ecosystem will facilitate the development and

promotion of cryptocurrencies backed by real-world assets. This approach aims to build trust and encourage increased crypto adoption in regions where the belief in physical assets as the ultimate investments prevails, as these cryptocurrencies offer users potential for security, price stability, and protection against inflation.

{"title": "Charting Your Course with the BasedBear Dao", "summary": "This letter extends an invitation to the BasedBear Dao, a social club for long-term investors seeking to disrupt traditional finance with a future-focused, yet grounded approach. Here, we combine bold vision with strategic rigor, fostering a community of \\"innovation bears\\" who achieve exponential gains through resilience and long-term perspective.", "description": "

Greetings, Fellow Investors,

Are you a long-term investor yearning to transcend the limitations of the status quo? Do you envision a future where audacious technological leaps are navigated with strategic foresight? If so, then the BasedBear Dao beckons.

The BasedBear Dao isn't just a social club; it's a gathering place for like-minded individuals who understand the power of patience and calculated risk. Here, the bear represents our core principle: unwavering strength and stability. We believe this \\"bear strength\\" is the bedrock for long-term success. We navigate the market with a keen eye for strategic opportunities, fostering resilience and honing our acumen to achieve lofty goals.

Membership within the BasedBear Dao unlocks a unique ecosystem:

  1. A network of experienced long-term investors: Share insights, learn from one another's triumphs and challenges, and build a supportive network.

  2. Collaborative investment discussions: Explore new opportunities, vet ideas, and develop a robust long-term strategy with the collective wisdom of the group.

The BasedBear Dao isn't about chasing short-term gains. We're about building a solid financial future with well-defined strategies and unwavering focus. We believe that calculated risks and a long-term perspective pave the way for true financial freedom.

If you're ready to join a community of like-minded investors and build long-term wealth on a foundation of strength and resilience, we invite you to explore the BasedBear Dao further.

{"title": "Proposal to Submit for Polygon Grants", "summary": "This Proposal for Grant Services (“Proposal”) authorizes GoBlock USA LLC, with Christopher Davis (“Chris”) as its member, and Aaron Tarnowski (“Aaron”), an individual, both working cooperatively, and each acting as independent contractors, and not in the capacity of Traxa council members, to perform the Services listed below on behalf of Traxa DAO, LLC (“Traxa”), and to receive the Professional Services Fees listed below for those Services.", "description": "

This Proposal for Grant Services (“Proposal”) authorizes GoBlock USA LLC, with Christopher Davis (“Chris”) as its member, and Aaron Tarnowski (“Aaron”), an individual, both working cooperatively, and each acting as independent contractors, and not in the capacity of Traxa council members, to perform the Services listed below on behalf of Traxa DAO, LLC (“Traxa”), and to receive the Professional Services Fees listed below for those Services.

Services

Chris and Aaron will investigate the Polygon grant system and if, at the sole discretion of Aaron and Chris, that investigation is found to yield a high probability of success, they will submit information on behalf of Traxa to obtain a grant from Polygon, as their time permits, and within no defined timeline, and as described at this link:

https://polygon.technology/village/grants

These Services will occur in two phases:

Phase 1 – obtaining contacts within Polygon, understanding the grant process, and determining the probability of success.  If Chris and Aaron feel there is a high probability of success, then they will proceed to Phase 2.  Else, the Services will stop here.

Phase 2 – compiling and submitting the information to obtain the grant,

Any other Services not described here are not in scope.  Any expansion of Services would be additional scope and incur additional Fees, both of which would be agreed in advance before undertaking that additional scope.

Professional Services Fees

Chris will bill all hours incurred at a rate $350 USD per hour.

Aaron will bill all hours incurred at a rate $250 USD per hour.

Traxa will receive at minimum 40% of awarded grant money from the Services. The following breakdown represents how all parties will split the grant money.

At the completion of the Services, Chris and Aaron will independently submit Invoices for Billable Dollars (defined as hours incurred multiplied by USD rate per hour) to the Traxa council.  Each Invoice will include a detailed accounting of the Billable Dollars including a record, by day, of daily hours incurred and a description of the activity for that day. The Invoices will be issued in USD and Traxa will pay the invoices in USD.

If Traxa is awarded grant money from the Services, and upon receipt of that grant money by Traxa, the Invoices to Chris and Aaron will be paid within 10 calendar days by check or electronic transfer.

If the Combined Billable Dollars of both Invoices is greater than 60% of the grant money awarded to Traxa, then the combined Professional Services Fees paid will equal 60% of the actual grant money awarded.  In this case, the Fees will be paid to Chris and Aaron according to the following:

Chris = (Chris’ Billable Dollars / Combined Billable Dollars) * 60% of grant money awarded

Aaron = (Aaron’s Billable Dollars / Combined Billable Dollars) * 60% of grant money awarded

Traxa will retain the remaining 40% of the grant money awarded with no further recourse for collection of payment by Chris or Aaron.

If Traxa is not awarded any grant money, then payment for the Invoices will be declined by Traxa with no further recourse for collection of payment by Chris or Aaron.

Out-of-pocket expenses incurred by Chris or Aaron, if preapproved by the council in advance, will be Invoiced as incurred, at actual cost with no markup, and with all receipts in support.

Understanding that it may be some time before Traxa receives grant money, this agreement to pay Chris and Aaron has no termination date, and survives the existence of Traxa in all cases.  In the case of the dissolution of Traxa and if post-dissolution the council members are still able to claim an awarded grant money, they agree to a personal recourse liability for the value of the Invoices if Traxa is awarded grant money.

Independent Contractor and Agent

It is expressly understood and agreed that Chris and Aaron are, and shall at all times be deemed to be, an independent contractor(s) to Traxa for the performing of Services as defined by the scope of work listed in this Proposal.

Chris and Aaron may represent themselves as Agents of Traxa DAO LLC to other parties when performing the Services.

Nothing contained in this Proposal shall be deemed to preclude Chris and Aaron from providing consulting or other services for others.

Confidentiality and Work Product

All deliverables of the Services are considered Confidential Information.  Upon payment of the Invoices, the deliverables will be solely owned by Traxa.

Addendums for Future Grant or Investment Submission Services

If the Traxa council decides to engage Chris and Aaron in future Services to obtain grant or other investment money, in addition to the Services listed here, this Proposal may be amended in writing through an Addendum that references this Proposal. Addendums will document the additional Services, and any new or different terms.  All language in this Proposal will apply to all future referenced Addendum unless language in that Addendum is specifically noted as a change from this Proposal.  Each Addendum will be voted on and validated in the same manner as listed in the next section of this Proposal.

Electronic Agreement and Recourse Liability

This Proposal, and any future Addendums, will be documented in the Aragon system and voted on using that Aragon system at the following address:

https://app.aragon.org/#/daos/polygon/0xf8b18bba2c235b887b79f1f3ac134de19fab8924/dashboard

Upon 2/3rds of council members voting in the affirmative, the Proposal and Addendums will be valid and in effect.  The electronic nature of this voting and validation in no way alters the legal status of the agreements herein, and all council members voting in the affirmative personally attest to the legal validity of the Proposal and Addendums.

All council members voting in the affirmative agree to their personal recourse liability for any unpaid Invoices under this Proposal and Addendums upon receipt of awarded grant money.

It is understood that Chris and Aaron will be voting on the Proposal and Addendums as Traxa council members, and as independent contractors. Therefore, their vote in the affirmative binds them to the Proposal and Addendums in both capacities.

In the case of a tie vote or a vote to decline the Proposal or Addendums, the Traxa council may submit a request for clarification, and an updated Proposal, or its Addendum, will be provided by Chris and Aaron.

Venue is the state of Wyoming.

{"title": "Additional Proposal: Governance Structure Adjustment with MultiSig Holder Cap", "summary": "MultiSig Holder Cap:\\n\\nObjective: It is proposed that the number of MultiSig wallets be capped at 20 to foster efficient and dynamic governance within Guilds United DAO.", "description": "

  • Implementation: Upon reaching this cap, the introduction of any additional wallet will require a vote. The DAO members will determine if an existing MultiSig holder will step down to accommodate the new member, ensuring we adhere to the 20-wallet maximum.

  • Voting Majority for Execution: Crucial to our decision-making process is the majority vote requirement. For any execution to pass, more than half of the current MultiSig holders must approve. For instance, if we have 14 members, a majority would be 8 approvals; for 18 members, 10 approvals would be needed, and so on. This structure is designed to ensure decisive action represents a consensus among the MultiSig holders, reflecting the collective will.

  • Rationale: The establishment of a cap aims to balance the agility of our decision-making process with the opportunity to introduce new perspectives, preserving the effectiveness and vitality of Guilds United's governance.

    Currently we are at 14 members and the approval is set at 7, When a new member is voted in as a MultiSig holder (The 15th member) the threshold will need to be increased to 8 as it was previously overlooked when adding in the 14th member. This needs to be done when a new members wallet is added to the DAO.

This additional proposal is put forward to ensure our governance remains robust and representative of our community's evolving needs and aspirations.

Your support for this proposal is invaluable, and we await your affirmative vote.

Thank you.

", "resourcesJson": "[]"}

{"title": "Proposal for Revamping Guilds United Leveling System within Discord", "summary": "Guilds United, within Earth 2, aims to maintain an active, engaging community. We propose modifications to our leveling system to enhance the value of the Pioneer role, ensuring it represents a significant contribution to our community.\\n\\nThe current system allows members to attain the Pioneer role at level 10, mainly through general chat. This level of accessibility may not reflect the depth of engagement the role is meant to represent.", "description": "

Proposed Adjustments to the Leveling System:

  1. Elevate XP Threshold: Raise the level requirement for the Pioneer role to level 20, demanding more substantial time and participation investment from members.

  2. Restrict XP Gains: Limit XP gains to specific channels that encourage valuable contributions, while disabling them in the general chat to emphasize quality engagement.

  3. Incentivize Productive Participation: Assign higher XP rates to channels focused on projects, discussions, or events that align with our community's goals.

  4. Implement Cool-down Periods: Introduce cool-down timers to mitigate XP exploitation and ensure XP reflects true community engagement.

  5. Reward Specific Contributions: Offer XP bonuses for participation in events, contribution to projects, and other significant community activities.

  6. Introduce Role-Specific Challenges: Create challenges for members aiming for the Pioneer role, promoting diverse and impactful contributions.

  7. Automatic Role Upgrade with Quality Review: Upon reaching level 20, members will automatically be upgraded to the Pioneer role. However, DAO members will then review the quality of their contributions to the Guilds United Hub. This review aims to ensure that the attainment of level 20 reflects positive and constructive engagement, rather than negativity or poor-quality participation.

  8. Iterative Feedback Loop: Actively seek and incorporate member feedback to refine the leveling system, ensuring it aligns with the evolving dynamics of the community.

Request for Approval: We request the approval of the MultiSig holders to implement these changes. This will enhance the integrity of the Pioneer role, encouraging a more robust and positively engaged community in Guilds United.

Conclusion: Adopting these proposals will solidify the Pioneer role as a symbol of excellence in engagement within Guilds United, fostering a community that is both vibrant and committed.

Thank you for considering this proposal.

", "resourcesJson": "[]"}

{"title": "Guilds United Logo", "summary": "Proposal for the Adoption and Use of the Guilds United Logo with Augmented Reality Functionality", "description": "

Introduction:

The Guilds United logo is an emblem of our collective identity within the Earth 2 metaverse. This proposal is to formalize the significance, augmented reality functionality, and the materialistic narrative behind our logo, following its presentation and conditional acceptance by the Earth 2 Community Manager for non-commercial use.

Design Approval and Usage Terms:

On January 5, 2024, we crafted a logo symbolizing our alliance and presented it to the Earth 2 Community Manager. On January 8, 2024, our use of the Earth 2 emblem within our logo was conditionally approved, provided it is restricted to non-commercial purposes.

Logo Composition and Significance:

- AR Functionality: The logo is designed using Shapr3D, a testament to our innovative spirit, imbuing the emblem with augmented reality (AR) capabilities that reveal a three-dimensional character in virtual space.

- Artisanal Material Narrative: Envisioned with a blend of imagined materials, the outermost circle of partially transparent blue glass represents the purity and clarity of our mission. It is complemented by the inner elements of titanium and platinum, meticulously forged by the Cymasters, signifying our commitment to strength and resilience.

- Navigational Features: The logo incorporates a 360-degree navigational scale, embodying our mission to provide direction and purpose within Earth 2. The arms of the logo function as a compass, offering both orientation and distance measurement, reflecting our role as guides and pioneers in the metaverse.

- Multiplication and Time Representation: The 'X' on one of the rings facilitates distance calculations, transforming the logo into a practical tool for traversal in Earth 2. The 24-hour clock ring symbolizes the passage of time and the enduring nature of our endeavor.

- Symbolism of Fairness: The central scales, crafted from titanium, stand for the balance and equality that Guilds United upholds. The 'GU' at the heart of the logo reaffirms our unity and commitment to collective progress.

- Cymasters Endorsement: With the Cymasters' signature from Gibraltar, the logo carries the seal of our creators, ensuring authenticity and solidarity.

Proposal for Use:

We propose the formal adoption of the Guilds United logo for all community interactions within Earth 2, adhering to the non-commercial restrictions outlined by the Community Manager.

Conclusion:

The Guilds United logo stands as a symbol of innovation, community, and the guiding principles that unite us. Through this proposal, we seek to cement its role as the visual and functional standard-bearer of our shared values and aspirations.

Your favourable consideration and vote for this proposal are highly anticipated and appreciated.

", "resourcesJson": "[{\\"name\\":\\"Guilds United Logo\\",\\"url\\":\\"https://metahome.limited/images/GuildsUnited-Logo.png\\"}]"}

{"title": "AIP-02: Validator Performance Management Proposal", "summary": "Proposes a framework that will allow further decentralization of the network by means of validator self-regulation", "description": "### Abstract\\n\\n    In its current state, the PoS validator network is not entirely permissionless in that the previously-selected set of validators has largely persisted since testnet. \\n    \\n    Following the spirit of gradual decentralization, certain steps need to be taken with the end state being the community assuming care for the network.\\n    \\n    ### Motivation \\n    \\n    To arrive at a state of absolute decentralized self-governance, as was mentioned back in the [mainnet announcement](https://blog.aragon.technology/mainnet-is-going-live-announcing-the-launch-sequence-dao-network/), the validators will have to self-regulate network participation to an agreed set of parameters.\\n    \\n    Following on from the considerations and feedback of validators on the forum and elsewhere, the Aragon Governance Team wants to now put forward the following proposal for validators to accept or reject.\\n    \\n    ### Rationale\\n    \\n    Self-regulation in this context refers to setting and administering conditions for the admission, participation, and if necessary, forced exit of validators from the active set - with the last part formalizing previously-achieved consensus on the subject.\\n    \\n    Successfully engaging in self-regulation requires setting parameters with fair, transparent, and self-enforcing standards for:\\n    \\n    1. Measuring performance;\\n    2. Compliance with conditions of participation;\\n    3. Choosing and acting on remedial measures; and\\n    4. Addressing compliance breaches if and when they arise.\\n    \\n    Under the proposed parameters a validator who underperforms the benchmarks for 700 checkpoints will have a second 700 checkpoint period to correct the deficiency before a process to unbound its stake is implemented.\\n    \\n    The parameters in Part A and Part B were chosen following feedback from the validator community specifically and the Aragon community at large. \\n    \\n    If adopted, the framework would allow further decentralization of the network by means of validator self-regulation. \\n    \\n    As a result of the increased amount of validator churn that may occur as a result of Part A and B, Part C details the roadmap and process for which new validators would be admitted into the set in a way that promotes network health and aligns with the notion of gradual decentralization.\\n    \\n    In addition to the above, Part D proposes to increase the validator set from 100 to 105. This would increase the economic security and decentralization of the network. \\n    \\n    ### Specification\\n    \\n    ### **Part A**\\n    \\n    Here we propose a potential framework to manage validator performance through a self-enforced performance standard across the network.\\n    \\n    #### **1. Proposed Potential Parameters**\\n    \\n    Measure individual validator performance based upon checkpoints signed over a specified time period, performed on a rolling basis at each new checkpoint. Then measure this against a benchmark of the total network performance in that same time period, as detailed below:\\n    \\n    > * Monitoring Period (“MP\\") = previous 700 checkpoints, updated every new checkpoint.\\n    > * Take % of checkpoints signed by each validator in the MP, combine all % values and find the median average. \\n    > * Multiply median average by an agreed multiple = Performance Benchmark (PB).\\n    > * At each checkpoint, calculate the % of checkpoints signed in the MP by individual validators and measure against the PB.\\n    \\n    #### **2. Performance Benchmark**\\n    \\n    In order to transition validators into the process requiring satisfaction of the proposed potential parameters, there will be a slightly lower benchmark for the first ~2 months, while validators become accustomed to the parameters. \\n    \\n    > * PB1 → 95% of median average of last 700 checkpoints signed by validator set (first 2,800 checkpoints)\\n    > * PB2 → 98% of median average of last checkpoints signed by validator set (continues thereafter)\\n    \\n    #### **3. Validator Performance Dashboard**\\n    \\n    If validators adopt this proposal, then a public dashboard would be developed to provide live monitoring of individual and overall validator performance. \\n    \\n    The dashboard would serve the functions listed below.\\n    \\n    At launch:\\n    \\n    * Provide real time visual representations of validator performance telemetry.\\n    * Display validator notices and updates on their status, either meeting performance benchmarks or being deficient.\\n    \\n    In a future release:\\n    \\n    * Provide direct notifications to validators, alerting them to their deficient status.\\n    \\n    #### **4. Notice to Validators**\\n    \\n    If the validators adopt this proposal, after the MVP dashboard is developed and deployed, validators will be given 24 hours notice of the commencement of performance monitoring, with the data set beginning 24 hours after the notice is sent. \\n    \\n    ### Part B\\n    \\n    If the validators adopt this proposal, then any validator that falls below any performance benchmark would enter the process below:\\n    \\n    #### **1. Remedial measures and corrective action**\\n    \\n    Deficient Validator Process:\\n    \\n    > * If validator <PB in the MP → Grace Period (“GP”).\\n    > * If validator in GP <PB after 700 checkpoints → Notice of Deficiency (“NOD”), enters into Grace Period 2 (“GP2”).\\n    > * If validator in GP2 <PB after 700 checkpoints → Final Notice (“FN”), validator will be unstaked with no further resource. \\n    \\n    Definitions:\\n    \\n    The GP is 700 checkpoints long and allows a validator time to bring its performance back above the PB. If the deficiency is corrected within the GP, then there would be no further action.\\n    \\n    Failure to become compliant in the GP would result in issuance of a public NOD from the Performance Monitor (“PM”). The operator would have a 700 checkpoint period to correct the deficiency in GP2. If the deficiency is corrected within the NOD period, then there would be no further action, however the NOD would remain public.\\n    \\n    Failure to become compliant in the GP2 would result in issuance of a FN of the intent of the community to implement a forced exit procedure by offboarding the validator from the network by unbonding their stake.\\n    \\n    #### **2. Forced Unstaking**\\n    \\n    The unstaking of the deficient validator would be done as follows:\\n    \\n    Call `ForceUnstake` function in Aragon Commitchain Contract: \\n    \\n    * 0xFa7D2a996aC6350f4b56C043112Da0366a59b74c \\n    \\n    ### **Part C**\\n    \\n    #### **1. Validator Admission**\\n    \\n    To repopulate the validator slots that may become vacant as a result of the forced unstaking detailed in Part B, below is the proposed roadmap for the admission and onboarding of new validators into the set:\\n    \\n    * Phase 1 (current) - The multi-sig holders would set the admission parameters and choose the validators.\\n    * Phase 2 (next step) - The governance and validator communities set and approve the parameters, and the multi-sig holders implement those parameters.\\n    * Phase 3 (end state) - The governance and validator communities set the parameters and admit and onboard validators themselves.\\n    \\n    #### **2. Irregular Joining**\\n    \\n    Joining the validator network consists of two steps: admission and onboarding. These steps and specific parameters relating to each will ultimately be controlled by the governance community. This community approach enhances network security with a level of certainty about the intent of the actors trying to join the network, and introduces social ramifications, which can ensure proper functioning of the network, e.g., validator onboarding and education, participation in off-chain and on-chain governance, efficient communication on critical updates and/or node performance issues.\\n    \\n    Irregular joining occurs when a new validator joins the network, but does not follow or circumvents the community-approved admissions and onboarding process. Bot sniping of a node is an example of an instance of irregular joining.\\n    \\n    As irregularly-joining validators do not participate in the onboarding process, they cause massive issues for the network health. In particular, the inability to communicate on critical updates and/or node performance issues is probledao.\\n    \\n    As a result, we propose if this proposal is adopted:\\n    \\n    1. Participation in the admission process (outlined in Part C) will be viewed as obligatory;\\n    2. Any future participants who circumvent the process will be unstaked from the network;\\n    3. The minimum $DAOstaked will be increased from 1 to 10,000 in order to deter snipers. \\n    \\n    ### **Part D**\\n    \\n    #### **1. Increasing the Validator Set**\\n    \\n    At times, a situation may arise in which an underperforming validator has already been offboarded, following a Final Notice, but a new one has not yet been onboarded to the network. This essentially means that the active validator count may occasionally drop below 100. Additionally, validators that underperform for prolonged periods of time effectively reduce the active set. \\n    \\n    In order to maintain network integrity and guarantee an active set of 100 efficient validators, we propose increasing the maximum validator count to 105 from 100.\\n    \\n    As the network has matured and seen wide adoption, it seems warranted to provide new validator slots for added decentralization and security. A conservative increase of 5 slots seems like it would serve as a good starting point.\\n    \\n    ---\\n    \\n    ### References\\n    \\n    [Aragon Forum Discussion](https://forum.aragon.technology/t/aip-4-validator-performance-management/9956/24)", "resourcesJson": "[{\\"name\\":\\"Github\\",\\"url\\":\\"https://github.com/xavikh/fantastic-aip/blob/main/AIPs/AIP-02.md\\"}]"}

{"title": "AIP-04: Validator Performance Management Proposal", "summary": "Proposes a framework that will allow further decentralization of the network by means of validator self-regulation", "description": "### Abstract\\n\\nIn its current state, the PoS validator network is not entirely permissionless in that the previously-selected set of validators has largely persisted since testnet. \\n\\nFollowing the spirit of gradual decentralization, certain steps need to be taken with the end state being the community assuming care for the network.\\n\\n### Motivation \\n\\nTo arrive at a state of absolute decentralized self-governance, as was mentioned back in the [mainnet announcement](https://blog.aragon.technology/mainnet-is-going-live-announcing-the-launch-sequence-dao-network/), the validators will have to self-regulate network participation to an agreed set of parameters.\\n\\nFollowing on from the considerations and feedback of validators on the forum and elsewhere, the Aragon Governance Team wants to now put forward the following proposal for validators to accept or reject.\\n\\n### Rationale\\n\\nSelf-regulation in this context refers to setting and administering conditions for the admission, participation, and if necessary, forced exit of validators from the active set - with the last part formalizing previously-achieved consensus on the subject.\\n\\nSuccessfully engaging in self-regulation requires setting parameters with fair, transparent, and self-enforcing standards for:\\n\\n1. Measuring performance;\\n2. Compliance with conditions of participation;\\n3. Choosing and acting on remedial measures; and\\n4. Addressing compliance breaches if and when they arise.\\n    ", "resourcesJson": "[{\\"name\\":\\"Github\\",\\"url\\":\\"https://github.com/xavikh/fantastic-aip/blob/main/AIPs/AIP-04.md\\"}]"}

Measuring performance;\\n2. Compliance with conditions of participation;\\n3. Choosing and acting on remedial measures; and\\n4. Addressing compliance breaches if and when they arise.\\n\\nUnder the proposed parameters a validator who underperforms the benchmarks for 700 checkpoints will have a second 700 checkpoint period to correct the deficiency before a process to unbound its stake is implemented.\\n\\nThe parameters in Part A and Part B were chosen following feedback from the validator community specifically and the Aragon community at large. \\n\\nIf adopted, the framework would allow further decentralization of the network by means of validator self-regulation. \\n\\nAs a result of the increased amount of validator churn that may occur as a result of Part A and B, Part C details the roadmap and process for which new validators would be admitted into the set in a way that promotes network health and aligns with the notion of gradual decentralization.\\n\\nIn addition to the above, Part D proposes to increase the validator set from 100 to 105.
