{"title": "Auction Mechanism - A Mechanism to replace bad and under-performing validators", "summary": "Proposes a mechanism to replace under-performing validators", "description": "## Abstract:\\n\\nThe Aragon chain has a soft limit of 100 validators securing the network. Once 100 validators have been onboarded to secure the network, there should be a way to add newer and highly proficient validators to the network to replace under-performing and bad validators. This particular mechanism of adding new validators is called the Auction Mechanism.\\n\\n### Motivation:\\n\\nA Aragon (Dao) started the Mainnet a year ago, we have always wanted to onboard highly proficient validators to secure the network. Over the period of 1 year, we have managed to onboard proficient and ethical validators to the network, however, there are a few under-performing validators that have been observed during this period. The Auction Mechanism is a way to replace under-performing and bad validators with proficient and technical ones. Aragon aims to have all validators with high uptime and high performing and engaging validators to secure the Aragon chain.\\n\\n### Specification:\\n\\nThe Auction functionality is currently disabled through Aragon's Smart Contracts. Through the Auction Mechanism, a Validator will go through a bidding Mechanism where the newer validator has to bid higher than the current validator that is occupying the slot. Once the Auction period is over, whoever has the highest bid at the end of it becomes the validator.\\n\\nTo understand this in more detail I will add some specifics.\\n\\nA Dynasty is formed by adding a chunk of checkpoints together. 1 Dynasty on the Aragon chain equals 80 checkpoints. A Dynasty is only a matter of reference. It does not perform any special duties on the chain.\\n\\nDuring a Dynasty, 20 checkpoints are allotted for Auctions. Only during these 20 checkpoints is where validators will be under auction. The auction period will last only for 20 checkpoints. Right now each checkpoint is submitted to Ethereum approximately every 30 mins.\\n\\nValidators who are in the auction pool are selected as per this logic\\n\\n```\\nrequire(\\n            (_currentEpoch.sub(validators[validatorId].activationEpoch) % dynasty.add(auctionPeriod)) < auctionPeriod,\\n            \\"Invalid auction period\\"\\n        );\\n\\n```\\n\\nWhere for example, your validator activation checkpoint (epoch) is 1 then windows for bidding are `21-40, 101-120`, and so on. Technically speaking, as a validator, you wouldn't need to calculate this and keep this ready. The Staking UI will ensure that each validator would know when they are in an auction period or not.\\n\\nOnce a validator is in the auction pool, it will be open to bids. Any new/provisional validator can bid on any of the validators that are in the auction pool. To make a successful bid, they will have to bid higher than the \\"**Overall Stake**\\" of a validator and not just the \\"**Self Stake**\\" of the validator.\\n\\nOnce the validator successfully bids a higher bid than the overall stake of a current validator, the current validator will be notified on the Staking UI and they will have to ensure that, that if they want to keep their place as a validator they will need to bid higher than the current bid.\\n\\n*Auction Pool = Validators that are currently under auction*\\n\\nFor example, If a validator's self stake is 100 and overall stake is 1000. A new validator bids 2000 for his slot in the auction. For the current validator to outbid him, they will have to raise their stake by 901, thus their overall stake would increase and then the current validator becomes the highest bidder.\\n\\n*Note: The current validator only needs to increase the overall stake value during the bidding process and not necessarily compare it with the `self stake`.*\\n\\nDepending on the Bid status at the end of the Auction period, whoever has the highest bid gets to keep the validator slot. If a new validator's bid was the highest at the end of the auction period, then that validator will replace the old validator with `Confirming Bid`. Only once the new validator Confirms the Bid, only then he will be part of the active `validator set`. Until then he will not be part of it.\\n\\nIf there were no bids on any validator during the auction period, then the validator resumes normal operations.\\n\\n**Note: During the auction period, a validator that is currently under auction still assumes normal operations like creating blocks and signing checkpoints.**\\n\\nThis cycle continues for every dynasty where various validators will be under auction at a given point in time.\\n\\nFor more details on the Auction Code you can check it out here:\\n\\n**Auction Smart Contract**: [https://github.com/test/contracts/blob/v0.3.0-backport/contracts/staking/stakeManager/StakeManagerExtension.sol](https://github.com/test/contracts/blob/v0.3.0-backport/contracts/staking/stakeManager/StakeManagerExtension.sol)\\n\\n### Rationale:\\n\\nThe rationale behind this proposal is very simple. We at Aragon strive to build the best products and infrastructure to ensure Dapps and users get the best experience. In order of that, we also need to ensure that the Aragon chain is secured by high-performing and efficient validators too.\\n\\nIf the chain is only secured by a few highly performing validators then it isn't secure enough. The Auction mechanism is a way to remediate this problem, where new and highly performing validators can replace underperforming and bad validators through a secure and just process.\\n\\n### Backwards Compatibility:\\n\\nTo enable Auction, it is merely a config change in the Smart Contracts deployed by Aragon on Ethereum. If this proposal is met with a consensus, then the owner of the Smart Contracts will only need to make a small transaction on Ethereum to enable Auctions. Similarly, if the auction mechanism is disrupting the network in any way, we can also go ahead and disable the auction through the contracts.\\n\\nIn the future, this would ideally be a governance vote for enabling or disabling auctions.\\n\\n### Security Considerations:\\n\\nAs far as we think, we don't see any security considerations involving enabling Auctions on the Chain. This would induce a lot of engagement and pro-activeness on the validator front to ensure that they are always updated about everything.\\n\\n---\\n\\n### References \\n\\n[Aragon Forum Discussion](https://forum.aragon.technology/t/aip-3-auction-mechansim-a-mechanism-to-replace-bad-and-under-performing-validators/8270)\\n", "resourcesJson": "[{\\"name\\":\\"Forum\\",\\"url\\":\\"https://forum.aragon.technology/t/aip-3-auction-mechansim-a-mechanism-to-replace-bad-and-under-performing-validators/8270/6\\"},{\\"name\\":\\"Github\\",\\"url\\":\\"https://github.com/xavikh/fantastic-aip/blob/main/AIPs/AIP-03.md\\"}]"}

{"title": "Proposal to distribute and decentralize staking power across the Aragon chain", "summary": "Proposal to increase the minimum commission.", "description": "## Abstract:\\n\\nAragon has seen an explosion in staking in the over one year mainnet has been active. All 100 validator slots are now filled, but the distribution of delegated DAOis clustered around large validators with low or 0% commission. Unfortunately, this has caused centralization and puts the network at risk. By increasing the minimum commission, the validators, delegators, and the network can benefit:\\n\\n### Motivation:\\n\\nThe top 10 validators have received 82% of the total delegation. Out of those validators, only the Binance node has a commission greater than 4%, with 7 out of 10 having a 0% commission. These low commissions incentivize additional delegations, which further centralizes the network. 0% commission also removes incentive for validators to keep a high uptime, which further threatens network security.\\nIncreasing the minimum commission charged will give validators more incentive to utilize professional hardware and maintain the highest possible uptime. Increasing the minimum commission will also give delegators more nodes to choose from at the same reward level. This will certainly help to decentralize the network. A decentralized network is more secure, leading to safety of funds for both delegators and the Aragon network as a whole. Properly funding validators will allow nodes to invest in performant hardware, which will allow a greater uptime percentage. With greater uptime percentage, fewer checkpoints will be missed and that will translate into more block rewards for delegators.\\n\\n### Specification:\\n\\nCurrently, there is only a MAX_COMMISSION_RATE set to 100 in the DaoStake Manager 7 contract. A new contract would be required to include a MIN_COMMISSION_RATE, possibly as a configurable variable for easy update in the future. In the below code, this MIN_COMMISSION_RATE is created, along with the code enforcing the new minimum.\\n\\n```\\nuint256 constant MIN_COMMISSION_RATE = 5;\\n\\nuint256 constant MAX_COMMISSION_RATE = 100;\\n\\n...\\n\\nfunction updateCommissionRate(uint256 validatorId, uint256 newCommissionRate) external {\\n\\n...\\n\\nrequire(newCommissionRate >= MIN_COMMISSION_RATE, \\"Incorrect value\\");\\n\\nrequire(newCommissionRate <= MAX_COMMISSION_RATE, \\"Incorrect value\\");\\n\\nvalidators[validatorId].commissionRate = newCommissionRate;\\n\\n}\\n\\n```\\n\\nA minimum of a 5% commission was selected as enough room for the average validator to maintain their servers, but not too much that it starts to cut into delegators rewards. This is also in line with many other PoS blockchains, where 0% commission is not as common. Tezos’ bakers average 8-15%, Harmony One validators average 2.5-10%, Persistence One validators average 5-10%, and Solana validators average 10%, to name a few.\\n\\n### Rationale:\\n\\n**A common misconception about the Aragon network is that staking rewards are distributed when a validator creates a block or sends a checkpoint, and delegators will get more rewards by staking with larger validators. This isn’t completely accurate, as any validator that is online to validate a checkpoint will receive rewards. Rewards are sent to validators and delegators based on their total stake in the network. Therefore, outside of commission, the only variables in rewards are how much a delegator stake, and the uptime of the validator. Not only does staking with smaller validators provide the same rewards, it encourages better decentralization of the network.**\\n\\nOther options explored:\\n\\n- Putting a max cap on each validator’s total stake\\n- Decreasing rewards if a validator’s total stake surpasses a threshold\\n- Requiring a validator to stake a percentage of the total stake on their node, which incentivizes good behavior among larger validators\\n\\n### Backwards Compatibility:\\n\\nThere are no backwards compatibility issues in terms of functionality, as having a validator change their commission is already built in. However, the current contract would not be usable as there is no way to enforce a minimum commission, so a contract would need to be deployed.\\n\\n### **Security Considerations:**\\n\\nAlthough there is not much risk in implementing a minimum commission, any modification or new deployment of a contract inherits risks of bugs. Issues with the updateCommissionRate should only be limited to allowing validators to choose any number for their commission, and should not extend into other parts of the contract or Aragon ecosystem.\\n\\n---\\n\\n### References\\n\\n[Aragon Forum Discussion](https://forum.aragon.technology/t/aip-2-proposal-to-distribute-and-decentralize-staking-power-across-the-aragon-chain/219/1)\\n", "resourcesJson": "[{\\"name\\":\\"Forum\\",\\"url\\":\\"https://forum.aragon.technology/t/aip-2-proposal-to-distribute-and-decentralize-staking-power-across-the-aragon-chain/219/11\\"},{\\"name\\":\\"Github\\",\\"url\\":\\"https://github.com/xavikh/fantastic-aip/blob/main/AIPs/AIP-02.md\\"}]"}

{"title": "Aragon 2.0 Phase 0 - Frontier", "summary": "Specification of Aragon 2.0 - Phase 0", "description": "## Abstract\\n\\nThis proposal specifies Phase 0 of Aragon 2.0, a multi-phased upgrade to the Aragon Ecosystem. In Phase 0, the functioning of Aragon PoS and Aragon zkEVM are entirely unaffected for end users. Multiple changes are proposed to be implemented in the system smart contracts of these chains on Ethereum. In an homage to the original Ethereum release, we propose calling this initial phase of changes, “Frontier”. \\n\\nThe core of the proposed changes are:\\n\\n1.  The initiation of the ANT upgrade\\n2.  The upgrade from DAOto ANT as the native (gas) token for PoS\\n3.  The adoption of ANT as the staking token for Aragon PoS\\n4.  The launch of the Staking Layer and migration of Aragon public chains to consequently leverage it\\n    \\nWe propose a high-level outline for these changes, which should be deployed incrementally, with further detail on each coming through separate proposals to be published for community review.\\n\\n### Motivation\\n\\nAragon 2.0 envisions a network of interconnected ZK-powered L2 chains that, on aggregate, expand Ethereum blockspace and create the Value Layer of the Internet. This environment is seamlessly interoperable, offering **access to unified blockspace** across all Aragon chains as well as infinite scalability. \\n\\n**The end goal is to extend Ethereum blockspace in a fashion that more resembles a mesh network toantogy like the internet**. With ZK technology, Ethereum blockspace can scale to the size of the Internet for the first time in blockchain history.\\n\\nFrom the start, the Aragon ecosystem has been committed to sustainably scaling Ethereum, bringing the idea of credibly neutral, decentralized blockchains to developers of all skill levels, while also ensuring that these blockchains remain cheap and accessible for all users. \\n\\nThis proposal represents the next step in this journey, defining the initial tasks to bring the Aragon 2.0 vision to life.\\n\\n### Specification\\n\\nPhase 0 is specifically designed to ensure that there will be no required changes for end users and developers already on the PoS or zkEVM chains. All proposed adjustments are targeted toward the various contracts and systems controlling the Aragon 1.0 ecosystem on Ethereum. \\n\\nThe following milestones will make up Phase 0:\\n\\n#### ANT Upgrade\\n\\n-   Initiate upgrade of ANT, a technical upgrade to DAO, designed to power the Aragon 2.0 ecosystem.\\n-   Adopt ANT as the native (gas) token for Aragon PoS. This will be accomplished by an upgrade to the Plasma Bridge, changing the DAOwithdrawal logic to only output ANT.\\n-   Adopt ANT as the staking token for Aragon PoS.\\n-   Upgrade the Aragon PoS validator rewards from DAOto ANT.\\n  \\n#### Staking Layer\\n\\n-   Launch the Staking Layer, a series of smart contracts that enable validators in the Aragon ecosystem to use their stake to participate in multiple services across the Aragon 2.0 ecosystem such as Validation, Sequencing, Proving, and Data Availability services for a variety of chains. \\n-   Migrate Aragon PoS staking and validation to a new service on the Staking Layer.\\n-   Migrate validator rewards from the legacy Aragon PoS staking contract to the new PoS Staking Layer service.\\n\\n![](https://lh4.googleusercontent.com/U1IpTIuLkykLmYkvot6IBNdO1NY6h3Kj_mQA1rh4dLfhKbmYI_Ml-aeeEk2kcFsJUpXrgRrs114OY6s_-6v7O15u71qin6g2juF_EFLPuZZtFj9dzo-bheH_k4zmq6kwSqhtBhLmzKBM-b5PlPVwEb4)\\n\\n### Backward Compatibility\\n\\nThis proposal seeks to lay out a roadmap that includes backwards incompatibility through the introduction, deprecation, and modification of Aragon system smart contracts on the Ethereum mainnet. As a result, this proposal covers a proposed order of operations and directly informs the creation of future AIPs to implement the functionality laid out in the “Specification” section. Relevant parties should carefully consider the changes laid out in this post, and monitor the eventual AIPs detailing them further to be prepared for any incompatibilities that will be introduced.\\n\\n### Security Considerations\\n\\nAragon 2.0 is a significant technical upgrade of the Aragon system with broadscale changes to the PoS network, as well as the introduction of several novel systems. There are inherent risks in this migration, however, each component will undergo extensive testing, auditing, and public scrutiny prior to activation.  The staging of the AIPs and the methodical, piecemeal upgrade of the system is intended to ensure there is sufficient time for testing, auditing and scrutiny. \\n\\n### References\\n\\n-   [AIP-17: Aragon Ecosystem Token (ANT)](https://github.com/xavikh/fantastic-aip/blob/main/AIPs/AIP-17.md)\\n-   [AIP-19: Update Aragon PoS Native Token to ANT](https://github.com/xavikh/fantastic-aip/blob/main/AIPs/AIP-19.md)\\n\\n### Copyright\\n\\nAll copyrights and related rights in this work are waived under CC0 1.0 Universal.\\n\\n", "resourcesJson": "[{\\"name\\":\\"Forum\\",\\"url\\":\\"https://forum.aragon.technology/t/aip-18-aragon-2-0-phase-0-frontier/12913\\"},{\\"name\\":\\"Github\\",\\"url\\":\\"https://github.com/xavikh/fantastic-aip/blob/main/AIPs/AIP-18.md\\"}]"}

{"title": "Adjust ANT Total Supply", "summary": "Proposes burning ANT tokens corresponding to previously burned DAO", "description": "### Abstract\\n\\nIn response to community feedback, this AIP proposes that the `burn()` function be called on the migrator contract, introduced in [AIP-17](https://github.com/test/Aragon-Improvement-Proposals/blob/main/AIPs/AIP-17.md), in order to burn an amount of ANT equivalent to DAOin the addresses currently used to permanently remove DAOtokens from supply (ex: 0x000..dEaD) as well as other permanently inaccessible tokens, thus bringing the ANT circulating supply inline with the DAOsupply.\\n\\n### Motivation\\n\\n[AIP-17](https://github.com/test/Aragon-Improvement-Proposals/blob/main/AIPs/AIP-17.md), which initiates the ANT upgrade, proposes the minting of 10B ANT to the migrator contract which corresponds to the entire original supply of DAO. In order to accurately reflect the amount of previously burned DAOin the ANT supply, the authors propose the burning of corresponding ANT.\\n\\n### Specification\\n\\nCall `burn()` on the migrator contract with an amount reflective of the DAObalances of the following addresses:\\n\\n-   0x000000000000000000000000000000000000dEaD \\\\[DAOburn address\\\\] - 28,048,923.20944512682731682 DAO\\n-   0x7D1AfA7B718fb893dB30A3aBc0Cfc608AaCfeBB0 \\\\[DAOtoken address\\\\] - 479,955.33752218388182171 DAO\\n\\n*Exact values determined by the [Agra Hardfork](https://github.com/test/Aragon-Improvement-Proposals/blob/main/AIPs/AIP-28.md)*\\n\\n### Backward Compatibility\\n\\nThis change causes no identifiable backward incompatibilities. \\n\\n### Copyright\\n\\nAll copyrights and related rights in this work are waived under CC0 1.0 Universal.\\n", "resourcesJson": "[{\\"name\\":\\"Forum\\",\\"url\\":\\"https://forum.aragon.technology/t/aip-25-adjust-ant-total-supply/13008\\"},{\\"name\\":\\"Github\\",\\"url\\":\\"https://github.com/xavikh/fantastic-aip/blob/main/AIPs/AIP-25.md\\"}]"}

{"title": "Increase Max Code Size Limit to 32KB", "summary": "Proposal to increase Max Code Size Limit from 24KB to 32KB", "description": "### Abstract\\n\\nThis AIP proposes increasing the `MAX_CODE_SIZE` Limit set in [EIP-170](https://github.com/ethereum/EIPs/blob/master/EIPS/eip-170.md) from `0x6000 (2**14+2**13)` to `0x8000 (2**15)`.\\n\\n### Motivation\\n\\nThe author proposes raising the limit to `0x8000` to allow for the deployment of more complex contracts without needing to resort to alternative development patterns.\\n  \\n### Specification\\n\\nAt block `FORK_BLKNUM`, the `MAX_CODE_SIZE` limit changes from `0x6000` bytes to `0x8000` bytes. If contract creation initialization returns data with length of more than `0x8000` bytes, contract creation fails with an out of gas error.\\n\\n### Backward Compatibility\\n\\nThis change causes no identifiable backward incompatibilities.  \\n\\n### Security Considerations \\n\\nAdded in the Spurious Dragon Ethereum Hard Fork, `MAX_CODE_SIZE` was introduced to cap an attack vector where a quadratic action, O(n) of reading code from disk, was charged a constant gas. At the time the limit `0x6000` was set as there were no contracts of that size. Since that time, contracts have gotten more complex and frequently brush up against that limit, leading to the need to use various different methods of reducing contract size, some of which can introduce vulnerabilities.\\n\\nStorage mediums have gotten faster since this limit was introduced so fetching large data is easier. Furthermore, 32KB fits perfectly in two 16 KB I/O blocks or a single 32 KB I/O block.\\n\\n### References\\n\\n-   [EIP-170: Contract code size limit](https://github.com/ethereum/EIPs/blob/master/EIPS/eip-170.md)\\n  \\n### Copyright\\n\\nAll copyrights and related rights in this work are waived under CC0 1.0 Universal.  \\n", "resourcesJson": "[{\\"name\\":\\"Forum\\",\\"url\\":\\"https://forum.aragon.technology/t/aip-30-increase-max-code-size-limit-to-32kb/13266\\"},{\\"name\\":\\"Github\\",\\"url\\":\\"https://github.com/xavikh/fantastic-aip/blob/main/AIPs/AIP-30.md\\"}]"}

{"title": "Aragon 2.0 Phase 0 - Frontier", "summary": "Specification of Aragon 2.0 - Phase 0", "description": "### Abstract\\n\\nThis proposal specifies Phase 0 of Aragon 2.0, a multi-phased upgrade to the Aragon Ecosystem. In Phase 0, the functioning of Aragon PoS and Aragon zkEVM are entirely unaffected for end users. Multiple changes are proposed to be implemented in the system smart contracts of these chains on Ethereum. In an homage to the original Ethereum release, we propose calling this initial phase of changes, “Frontier”. \\n\\nThe core of the proposed changes are:\\n\\n1.  The initiation of the ANT upgrade\\n2.  The upgrade from DAOto ANT as the native (gas) token for PoS\\n3.  The adoption of ANT as the staking token for Aragon PoS\\n4.  The launch of the Staking Layer and migration of Aragon public chains to consequently leverage it\\n    \\nWe propose a high-level outline for these changes, which should be deployed incrementally, with further detail on each coming through separate proposals to be published for community review.\\n\\n### Motivation\\n\\nAragon 2.0 envisions a network of interconnected ZK-powered L2 chains that, on aggregate, expand Ethereum blockspace and create the Value Layer of the Internet. This environment is seamlessly interoperable, offering **access to unified blockspace** across all Aragon chains as well as infinite scalability. \\n\\n**The end goal is to extend Ethereum blockspace in a fashion that more resembles a mesh network toantogy like the internet**. With ZK technology, Ethereum blockspace can scale to the size of the Internet for the first time in blockchain history.\\n\\nFrom the start, the Aragon ecosystem has been committed to sustainably scaling Ethereum, bringing the idea of credibly neutral, decentralized blockchains to developers of all skill levels, while also ensuring that these blockchains remain cheap and accessible for all users. \\n\\nThis proposal represents the next step in this journey, defining the initial tasks to bring the Aragon 2.0 vision to life.\\n\\n### Specification\\n\\nPhase 0 is specifically designed to ensure that there will be no required changes for end users and developers already on the PoS or zkEVM chains. All proposed adjustments are targeted toward the various contracts and systems controlling the Aragon 1.0 ecosystem on Ethereum. \\n\\nThe following milestones will make up Phase 0:\\n\\n#### ANT Upgrade\\n\\n-   Initiate upgrade of ANT, a technical upgrade to DAO, designed to power the Aragon 2.0 ecosystem.\\n-   Adopt ANT as the native (gas) token for Aragon PoS. This will be accomplished by an upgrade to the Plasma Bridge, changing the DAOwithdrawal logic to only output ANT.\\n-   Adopt ANT as the staking token for Aragon PoS.\\n-   Upgrade the Aragon PoS validator rewards from DAOto ANT.\\n  \\n#### Staking Layer\\n\\n-   Launch the Staking Layer, a series of smart contracts that enable validators in the Aragon ecosystem to use their stake to participate in multiple services across the Aragon 2.0 ecosystem such as Validation, Sequencing, Proving, and Data Availability services for a variety of chains. \\n-   Migrate Aragon PoS staking and validation to a new service on the Staking Layer.\\n-   Migrate validator rewards from the legacy Aragon PoS staking contract to the new PoS Staking Layer service.\\n\\n![](https://lh4.googleusercontent.com/U1IpTIuLkykLmYkvot6IBNdO1NY6h3Kj_mQA1rh4dLfhKbmYI_Ml-aeeEk2kcFsJUpXrgRrs114OY6s_-6v7O15u71qin6g2juF_EFLPuZZtFj9dzo-bheH_k4zmq6kwSqhtBhLmzKBM-b5PlPVwEb4)\\n\\n### Backward Compatibility\\n\\nThis proposal seeks to lay out a roadmap that includes backwards incompatibility through the introduction, deprecation, and modification of Aragon system smart contracts on the Ethereum mainnet. As a result, this proposal covers a proposed order of operations and directly informs the creation of future AIPs to implement the functionality laid out in the “Specification” section. Relevant parties should carefully consider the changes laid out in this post, and monitor the eventual AIPs detailing them further to be prepared for any incompatibilities that will be introduced.\\n\\n### Security Considerations\\n\\nAragon 2.0 is a significant technical upgrade of the Aragon system with broadscale changes to the PoS network, as well as the introduction of several novel systems. There are inherent risks in this migration, however, each component will undergo extensive testing, auditing, and public scrutiny prior to activation.  The staging of the AIPs and the methodical, piecemeal upgrade of the system is intended to ensure there is sufficient time for testing, auditing and scrutiny. \\n\\n### References\\n\\n-   [AIP-17: Aragon Ecosystem Token (ANT)](https://github.com/test/Aragon-Improvement-Proposals/blob/main/AIPs/AIP-17.md)\\n-   [AIP-19: Update Aragon PoS Native Token to ANT](https://github.com/test/Aragon-Improvement-Proposals/blob/main/AIPs/AIP-19.md)\\n\\n### Copyright\\n\\nAll copyrights and related rights in this work are waived under CC0 1.0 Universal.\\n\\n", "resourcesJson": "[{\\"name\\":\\"Forum\\",\\"url\\":\\"https://forum.aragon.technology/t/aip-18-aragon-2-0-phase-0-frontier/12913\\"},{\\"name\\":\\"Github\\",\\"url\\":\\"https://github.com/xavikh/fantastic-aip/blob/main/AIPs/AIP-18.md\\"}]"}

{"title": "Transition from DAOto ANT Validator Rewards", "summary": "Proposes aligning the initial ANT Validator Rewards to current rewards schedule", "description": "### Abstract\\n\\nThis AIP proposes aligning the initial ANT Validator Rewards with the original [DAOValidator Rewards Schedule](https://wiki.aragon.technology/docs/pos/design/validator/rewards/#what-is-the-incentive), introduced during the launch of DAOin 2020, and ending in June 2025. The proposal is to continue, i.e. adhere to the current DAOreward schedule, and, once it ends (in June 2025), switch to the proposed Aragon 2.0 reward schedule. This would honor the social contract, i.e. public commitment, with regards to Aragon validator rewards, and enable smooth transition to Aragon 2.0.\\n  \\n### Motivation\\n\\n[AIP-17](https://github.com/test/Aragon-Improvement-Proposals/blob/main/AIPs/AIP-17.md), which initiates the ANT upgrade, introduces a 1% annual ANT emission for validator rewards.  The authors propose to complete the original commitments of the genesis DAOValidator Rewards Schedule, before commencing the aforementioned Aragon 2.0 validator reward schedule.\\n  \\nThe original and proposed outline for emissions can be seen in the table below. \\n\\n*Proposed Emissions Schedule With Comparison:*\\n\\n| Year    | Dates                         | Original Reward Pool | Original ANT Proposed Reward Pool | AIP-26 Proposed Reward Pool |\\n| ------- | ----------------------------- | -------------------- | --------------------------------- | --------------------------- |\\n| First   | June 2020 - June  2021        | 312,917,639          | -                                 | -                           |\\n| Second  | July 2021 - July 2022         | 275,625,675          | -                                 | -                           |\\n| Third   | July 2022 - July 2023         | 246,933,140 | - | - |\\n| Fourth | July 2023 - July 2024 | 204,303,976 |\\\\*134,700,000|\\\\*201,400,000|\\n| Fifth  | June  2024 - June 2025 | 160,219,840          | 101,000,000                       | 150,300,000                 |\\n| Sixth | June 2025 -  June 2026 | 0 | 101,000,000 | 103,530,000 |\\n\\n\\\\*estimated using 4 months of original schedule, 8 months of newly proposed schedule.\\n  \\n### Specification\\n\\nAdjust the implementation of DefaultEmissionManager.sol to follow the current DAOvalidator reward schedule, gradually reducing the reward emission via subsequent AIPs until June 2025. In July 2025,  commence the Aragon 2.0 schedule of 1% annual ANT emission for validator rewards.\\n\\n### Backward Compatibility\\n\\nThis change causes no identifiable backward incompatibilities.\\n  \\n### References \\n\\n-   [Original Distribution of Staking Token Rewards](https:forum.aragon.technology/t/an-update-on-distribution-of-staking-token-rewards/9654/)\\n    \\n### Copyright\\n\\nAll copyrights and related rights in this work are waived under CC0 1.0 Universal.\\n\\n\\n", "resourcesJson": "[{\\"name\\":\\"Github\\",\\"url\\":\\"https://github.com/xavikh/fantastic-aip/blob/main/AIPs/AIP-26.md\\"}]"}

{"title": "Aragon Ecosystem Token (ANT)", "summary": "Upgrade to DAOin the form of the Aragon Ecosystem Token (ANT)", "description": "## Abstract\\n\\nThis proposal describes an upgrade to DAO(0x7d1afa7b718fb893db30a3abc0cfc608aacfebb0) in the form of the Aragon Ecosystem Token (ANT). ANT is the upgraded native token of Aragon 2.0, along with its accompanying contracts and initial configurations to handle emission management and token migration. ANT allows for a one-to-one migration with DAOwith an initial supply of 10 billion ANT and yearly emission of 2% that will be equally distributed to stakers and a community treasury contract.\\n\\n\\n### Motivation\\n\\nThe token of the Aragon PoS chain, DAO, powered this single chain that allowed Ethereum to scale during times of high network congestion. Aragon 2.0 is the next iteration in the Ethereum scaling journey, with zero-knowledge proofs (“zk”) facilitating the expansion of Ethereum block-space across a multitude of L2 chains whilst also inheriting its security.  \\n\\nANT represents a next-generation token able to accommodate an ecosystem of zk-based Layer 2 chains by enabling the following utility:\\n\\n* Staking,\\n* Community ownership, and\\n* Governance.\\n\\n### Specification\\n\\n#### ANT Token Contract\\n\\nA token contract, ANT, is proposed, broadly based on the MIT-licensed OpenZeppelin ERC20 implementations which provide support for the default ERC20 standard, along with some non-standard functions for allowance modifications. The implementation also provides support for EIP-2612: Signature-Based Permit Approvals.\\n\\nUpon genesis, an initial supply of 10 billion will be minted to a migration contract (see below for details). Further mints may be called by an emission manager contract (see below for details). Emission Managers can be managed by Governance. An additional check-in mint function requires the mint rate to be less than 10 ANT per second.\\n\\nThe ANT token contract is not upgradeable, however Permit2 default approvals can be enabled or disabled by Governance and is enabled by default.\\n\\n```solidity\\n// SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT\\npragma solidity 0.8.21;\\n\\nimport {ERC20, ERC20Permit, IERC20} from \\"openzeppelin-contracts/contracts/token/ERC20/extensions/ERC20Permit.sol\\";\\nimport {AccessControlEnumerable} from \\"openzeppelin-contracts/contracts/access/AccessControlEnumerable.sol\\";\\nimport {IAragonEcosystemToken} from \\"./interfaces/IAragonEcosystemToken.sol\\";\\n\\n/// @title Aragon ERC20 token\\n/// @author Aragon Labs (@DhairyaSethi, @gretzke, @qedk, @simonDos)\\n/// @notice This is the Aragon ERC20 token contract on Ethereum L1\\n/// @dev The contract allows for a 1-to-1 representation between $ANT and $DAOand allows for additional emission based on hub and treasury requirements\\n/// @custom:security-contact security@aragon.technology\\ncontract AragonEcosystemToken is ERC20Permit, AccessControlEnumerable, IAragonEcosystemToken {\\n    bytes32 public constant EMISSION_ROLE = keccak256(\\"EMISSION_ROLE\\");\\n    bytes32 public constant CAP_MANAGER_ROLE = keccak256(\\"CAP_MANAGER_ROLE\\");\\n    bytes32 public constant PERMIT2_REVOKER_ROLE = keccak256(\\"PERMIT2_REVOKER_ROLE\\");\\n    address public constant PERMIT2 = 0x000000000022D473030F116dDEE9F6B43aC78BA3;\\n    uint256 public mintPerSecondCap = 13.37e18;\\n    uint256 public lastMint;\\n    bool public permit2Enabled;\\n\\n    constructor(\\n        address migration,\\n        address emissionManager,\\n        address protocolCouncil,\\n        address emergencyCouncil\\n    ) ERC20(\\"Aragon Ecosystem Token\\", \\"ANT\\") ERC20Permit(\\"Aragon Ecosystem Token\\") {\\n        if (\\n            migration == address(0) ||\\n            emissionManager == address(0) ||\\n            protocolCouncil == address(0) ||\\n            emergencyCouncil == address(0)\\n        ) revert InvalidAddress();\\n        _grantRole(DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE, protocolCouncil);\\n        _grantRole(EMISSION_ROLE, emissionManager);\\n        _grantRole(CAP_MANAGER_ROLE, protocolCouncil);\\n        _grantRole(PERMIT2_REVOKER_ROLE, protocolCouncil);\\n        _grantRole(PERMIT2_REVOKER_ROLE, emergencyCouncil);\\n        _mint(migration, 10_000_000_000e18);\\n        // we can safely set lastMint here since the emission manager is initialised after the token and won't hit the cap.\\n        lastMint = block.timestamp;\\n        _updatePermit2Allowance(true);\\n    }\\n\\n    /// @inheritdoc IAragonEcosystemToken\\n    function mint(address to, uint256 amount) external onlyRole(EMISSION_ROLE) {\\n        uint256 timeElapsedSinceLastMint = block.timestamp - lastMint;\\n        uint256 maxMint = timeElapsedSinceLastMint * mintPerSecondCap;\\n        if (amount > maxMint) revert MaxMintExceeded(maxMint, amount);\\n\\n        lastMint = block.timestamp;\\n        _mint(to, amount);\\n    }\\n\\n    /// @inheritdoc IAragonEcosystemToken\\n    function updateMintCap(uint256 newCap) external onlyRole(CAP_MANAGER_ROLE) {\\n        emit MintCapUpdated(mintPerSecondCap, newCap);\\n        mintPerSecondCap = newCap;\\n    }\\n\\n    /// @inheritdoc IAragonEcosystemToken\\n    function updatePermit2Allowance(bool enabled) external onlyRole(PERMIT2_REVOKER_ROLE) {\\n        _updatePermit2Allowance(enabled);\\n    }\\n\\n    /// @dev The permit2 contract has full approval by default. If the approval is revoked, it can still be manually approved.\\n    function allowance(address owner, address spender) public view override(ERC20, IERC20) returns (uint256) {\\n        if (spender == PERMIT2 && permit2Enabled) return type(uint256).max;\\n        return super.allowance(owner, spender);\\n    }\\n\\n    /// @inheritdoc IAragonEcosystemToken\\n    function version() external pure returns (string memory) {\\n        return \\"1.1.0\\";\\n    }\\n\\n    function _updatePermit2Allowance(bool enabled) private {\\n        emit Permit2AllowanceUpdated(enabled);\\n        permit2Enabled = enabled;\\n    }\\n}\\n```\\n\\n#### Migration Contract\\n\\nThe migration contract will accept two addresses, one for the DAOtoken and one for the ANT token respectively.\\n\\nThe contract shall receive the entire initial 10 billion ANT supply in order to allow 1-to-1 swaps for the entire 10 billion DAOsupply.\\n\\nGovernance can lock and unlock the ability to unmigrate ANT tokens back into an equivalent amount of DAO.\\n\\nThis contract is upgradeable via Governance with ANT tokens also being burnable via Governance. The initial implementation is described below.\\n\\n```solidity\\n// SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT\\npragma solidity 0.8.21;\\n\\nimport {IERC20} from \\"openzeppelin-contracts/contracts/token/ERC20/IERC20.sol\\";\\nimport {IERC20Permit} from \\"openzeppelin-contracts/contracts/token/ERC20/extensions/IERC20Permit.sol\\";\\nimport {SafeERC20} from \\"openzeppelin-contracts/contracts/token/ERC20/utils/SafeERC20.sol\\";\\nimport {Ownable2StepUpgradeable} from \\"openzeppelin-contracts-upgradeable/contracts/access/Ownable2StepUpgradeable.sol\\";\\nimport {IAragonMigration} from \\"./interfaces/IAragonMigration.sol\\";\\n\\n/// @title Aragon Migration\\n/// @author Aragon Labs (@DhairyaSethi, @gretzke, @qedk)\\n/// @notice This is the migration contract for Dao<-> Aragon ERC20 token on Ethereum L1\\n/// @dev The contract allows for a 1-to-1 conversion from $DAOinto $ANT and vice-versa\\n/// @custom:security-contact security@aragon.technology\\ncontract AragonMigration is Ownable2StepUpgradeable, IAragonMigration {\\n    using SafeERC20 for IERC20;\\n    using SafeERC20 for IERC20Permit;\\n\\n    IERC20 public immutable dao;\\n    IERC20 public aragon;\\n    bool public unmigrationLocked;\\n\\n    modifier onlyUnmigrationUnlocked() {\\n        if (unmigrationLocked) revert UnmigrationLocked();\\n        _;\\n    }\\n\\n    constructor(address dao_) {\\n        if (dao_ == address(0)) revert InvalidAddress();\\n        dao= IERC20(dao_);\\n        // so that the implementation contract cannot be initialized\\n        _disableInitializers();\\n    }\\n\\n    function initialize() external initializer {\\n        __Ownable_init();\\n    }\\n\\n    /// @notice This function allows owner/governance to set ANT token address *only once*\\n    /// @param aragon_ Address of deployed ANT token\\n    function setAragonToken(address aragon_) external onlyOwner {\\n        if (aragon_ == address(0) || address(aragon) != address(0)) revert InvalidAddressOrAlreadySet();\\n        aragon = IERC20(aragon_);\\n    }\\n\\n    /// @inheritdoc IAragonMigration\\n    function migrate(uint256 amount) external {\\n        emit Migrated(msg.sender, amount);\\n\\n        dao.safeTransferFrom(msg.sender, address(this), amount);\\n        aragon.safeTransfer(msg.sender, amount);\\n    }\\n\\n    /// @inheritdoc IAragonMigration\\n    function unmigrate(uint256 amount) external onlyUnmigrationUnlocked {\\n        emit Unmigrated(msg.sender, msg.sender, amount);\\n\\n        aragon.safeTransferFrom(msg.sender, address(this), amount);\\n        dao.safeTransfer(msg.sender, amount);\\n    }\\n\\n    /// @inheritdoc IAragonMigration\\n    function unmigrateTo(address recipient, uint256 amount) external onlyUnmigrationUnlocked {\\n        emit Unmigrated(msg.sender, recipient, amount);\\n\\n        aragon.safeTransferFrom(msg.sender, address(this), amount);\\n        dao.safeTransfer(recipient, amount);\\n    }\\n\\n    /// @inheritdoc IAragonMigration\\n    function unmigrateWithPermit(\\n        uint256 amount,\\n        uint256 deadline,\\n        uint8 v,\\n        bytes32 r,\\n        bytes32 s\\n    ) external onlyUnmigrationUnlocked {\\n        emit Unmigrated(msg.sender, msg.sender, amount);\\n\\n        IERC20Permit(address(aragon)).safePermit(msg.sender, address(this), amount, deadline, v, r, s);\\n        aragon.safeTransferFrom(msg.sender, address(this), amount);\\n        dao.safeTransfer(msg.sender, amount);\\n    }\\n\\n    /// @inheritdoc IAragonMigration\\n    function updateUnmigrationLock(bool unmigrationLocked_) external onlyOwner {\\n        emit UnmigrationLockUpdated(unmigrationLocked_);\\n        unmigrationLocked = unmigrationLocked_;\\n    }\\n\\n    /// @inheritdoc IAragonMigration\\n    function version() external pure returns (string memory) {\\n        return \\"1.1.0\\";\\n    }\\n\\n    /// @inheritdoc IAragonMigration\\n    function burn(uint256 amount) external onlyOwner {\\n        aragon.safeTransfer(0x000000000000000000000000000000000000dEaD, amount);\\n    }\\n\\n    uint256[49] private __gap;\\n}\\n```\\n\\n#### Emission Manager Contract\\n\\nThe emission manager contract has the exclusive ability to mint new ANT tokens and is implemented to distribute the tokens as follows:\\n\\n#### Validator Rewards \\n\\nEmissions are minted to the PoS staking contract (0x5e3ef299fddf15eaa0432e6e66473ace8c13d908) for staking rewards. After minting the appropriate amount of ANT, the migration contract will be used to ensure that staking rewards continue to be paid out in DAO, maximizing backward compatibility.\\n\\nYear 1-3:\\n\\nFor years 1-3, the ANT emissions schedule will follow the schedule defined in [AIP-26](https://github.com/xavikh/fantastic-aip/blob/main/AIPs/AIP-26.md).\\n\\nAfter Year 3:\\n\\n-   1% annual compounding. \\n\\n#### Community Treasury\\n\\n-   1% annual compounding emission is minted to a community treasury. Upon the deployment of contracts introduced in this proposal, a multi-signature wallet (0x2ff25495d77f380d5F65B95F103181aE8b1cf898) will be used to safeguard the funds until Community Board supervision is enacted.\\n    \\nA publicly callable function that is callable by any address can trigger the immediate minting of all vested emission.\\n\\nThis contract is upgradeable via Governance. The initial implementation is described below.\\n\\n```solidity\\n// SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT\\npragma solidity 0.8.21;\\n\\nimport {IAragonEcosystemToken} from \\"./interfaces/IAragonEcosystemToken.sol\\";\\nimport {IAragonMigration} from \\"./interfaces/IAragonMigration.sol\\";\\nimport {IDefaultEmissionManager} from \\"./interfaces/IDefaultEmissionManager.sol\\";\\nimport {Ownable2StepUpgradeable} from \\"openzeppelin-contracts-upgradeable/contracts/access/Ownable2StepUpgradeable.sol\\";\\nimport {SafeERC20} from \\"openzeppelin-contracts/contracts/token/ERC20/utils/SafeERC20.sol\\";\\nimport {PowUtil} from \\"./lib/PowUtil.sol\\";\\n\\n/// @title Default Emission Manager\\n/// @author Aragon Labs (@DhairyaSethi, @gretzke, @qedk, @simonDos)\\n/// @notice A default emission manager implementation for the Aragon ERC20 token contract on Ethereum L1\\n/// @dev The contract allows for a 3% mint per year (compounded). 2% staking layer and 1% treasury\\n/// @custom:security-contact security@aragon.technology\\ncontract DefaultEmissionManager is Ownable2StepUpgradeable, IDefaultEmissionManager {\\n    using SafeERC20 for IAragonEcosystemToken;\\n\\n    uint256 public constant INTEREST_PER_YEAR_LOG2 = 0.04264433740849372e18;\\n    uint256 public constant START_SUPPLY = 10_000_000_000e18;\\n    address private immutable DEPLOYER;\\n\\n    IAragonMigration public immutable migration;\\n    address public immutable stakeManager;\\n    address public immutable treasury;\\n\\n    IAragonEcosystemToken public token;\\n    uint256 public startTimestamp;\\n\\n    constructor(address migration_, address stakeManager_, address treasury_) {\\n        if (migration_ == address(0) || stakeManager_ == address(0) || treasury_ == address(0)) revert InvalidAddress();\\n        DEPLOYER = msg.sender;\\n        migration = IAragonMigration(migration_);\\n        stakeManager = stakeManager_;\\n        treasury = treasury_;\\n\\n        // so that the implementation contract cannot be initialized\\n        _disableInitializers();\\n    }\\n\\n    function initialize(address token_, address owner_) external initializer {\\n        // prevent front-running since we can't initialize on proxy deployment\\n        if (DEPLOYER != msg.sender) revert();\\n        if (token_ == address(0) || owner_ == address(0)) revert InvalidAddress();\\n\\n        token = IAragonEcosystemToken(token_);\\n        startTimestamp = block.timestamp;\\n\\n        assert(START_SUPPLY == token.totalSupply());\\n\\n        token.safeApprove(address(migration), type(uint256).max);\\n        // initial ownership setup bypassing 2 step ownership transfer process\\n        _transferOwnership(owner_);\\n    }\\n\\n    /// @inheritdoc IDefaultEmissionManager\\n    function mint() external {\\n        uint256 currentSupply = token.totalSupply(); // totalSupply after the last mint\\n        uint256 newSupply = inflatedSupplyAfter(\\n            block.timestamp - startTimestamp // time elapsed since deployment\\n        );\\n        uint256 amountToMint = newSupply - currentSupply;\\n        if (amountToMint == 0) return; // no minting required\\n\\n        uint256 treasuryAmt = amountToMint / 3;\\n        uint256 stakeManagerAmt = amountToMint - treasuryAmt;\\n\\n        emit TokenMint(amountToMint, msg.sender);\\n\\n        IAragonEcosystemToken _token = token;\\n        _token.mint(address(this), amountToMint);\\n        _token.safeTransfer(treasury, treasuryAmt);\\n        // backconvert ANT to DAObefore sending to StakeManager\\n        migration.unmigrateTo(stakeManager, stakeManagerAmt);\\n    }\\n\\n    /// @inheritdoc IDefaultEmissionManager\\n    function inflatedSupplyAfter(uint256 timeElapsed) public pure returns (uint256 supply) {\\n        uint256 supplyFactor = PowUtil.exp2((INTEREST_PER_YEAR_LOG2 * timeElapsed) / 365 days);\\n        supply = (supplyFactor * START_SUPPLY) / 1e18;\\n    }\\n\\n    /// @inheritdoc IDefaultEmissionManager\\n    function version() external pure returns (string memory) {\\n        return \\"1.1.0\\";\\n    }\\n\\n    uint256[48] private __gap;\\n}\\n```\\n### Backward Compatibility\\n\\nThis proposal does not change any active systems on either the Aragon PoS or Aragon zkEVM networks. All existing contracts will function as previously designed.\\n\\n### Security Considerations\\n\\nIn the event of an exploit, to prevent arbitrary amounts of ANT being minted, there is a variable hard cap on the maximum amount of tokens allowed to be minted per second. It is initialized at a maximum allowed number of 10 ANT to be minted per second.\\n\\nDue to the size, complexity, and importance of the proposed ANT contracts, there will be several internal and external code audits to ensure the security of the implementation detailed above.\\n\\n### References\\n\\n* [ANT Whitepaper](https://aragon.technology/papers/ant-whitepaper/)\\n* [ERC-20: Token Standard](https://github.com/ethereum/EIPs/blob/master/EIPS/eip-20.md/)\\n* [EIP-2612: Permit Extension for EIP-20 Signed Approvals](https://github.com/ethereum/EIPs/blob/master/EIPS/eip-2612.md)\\n\\n### Implementation \\n\\n* [https://github.com/0xAragon/ant-token](https://github.com/0xAragon/ant-token) \\n\\n### Copyright\\n\\nAll copyrights and related rights in this work are waived under CC0 1.0 Universal.\\n", "resourcesJson": "[{\\"name\\":\\"Forum\\",\\"url\\":\\"https://forum.aragon.technology/t/aip-17-aragon-ecosystem-token-ant/12912\\"},{\\"name\\":\\"Github\\",\\"url\\":\\"https://github.com/xavikh/fantastic-aip/blob/main/AIPs/AIP-17.md\\"}]"}

{"title": "Transition from DAOto ANT Validator Rewards", "summary": "Proposes aligning the initial ANT Validator Rewards to current rewards schedule", "description": "## Abstract\\n\\nThis AIP proposes aligning the initial ANT Validator Rewards with the original [DAOValidator Rewards Schedule](https://wiki.aragon.technology/docs/pos/design/validator/rewards/#what-is-the-incentive), introduced during the launch of DAOin 2020, and ending in June 2025. The proposal is to continue, i.e. adhere to the current DAOreward schedule, and, once it ends (in June 2025), switch to the proposed Aragon 2.0 reward schedule. This would honor the social contract, i.e. public commitment, with regards to Aragon validator rewards, and enable smooth transition to Aragon 2.0.\\n  \\n### Motivation\\n\\n[AIP-17](https://github.com/xavikh/fantastic-aip/blob/main/AIPs/AIP-17.md), which initiates the ANT upgrade, introduces a 1% annual ANT emission for validator rewards.  The authors propose to complete the original commitments of the genesis DAOValidator Rewards Schedule, before commencing the aforementioned Aragon 2.0 validator reward schedule.\\n  \\nThe original and proposed outline for emissions can be seen in the table below. \\n\\n*Proposed Emissions Schedule With Comparison:*\\n\\n| Year    | Dates                         | Original Reward Pool | Original ANT Proposed Reward Pool | AIP-26 Proposed Reward Pool |\\n| ------- | ----------------------------- | -------------------- | --------------------------------- | --------------------------- |\\n| First   | June 2020 - June  2021        | 312,917,639          | -                                 | -                           |\\n| Second  | July 2021 - July 2022         | 275,625,675          | -                                 | -                           |\\n| Third   | July 2022 - July 2023         | 246,933,140 | - | - |\\n| Fourth | July 2023 - July 2024 | 204,303,976 |\\\\*134,700,000|\\\\*201,400,000|\\n| Fifth  | June  2024 - June 2025 | 160,219,840          | 101,000,000                       | 150,300,000                 |\\n| Sixth | June 2025 -  June 2026 | 0 | 101,000,000 | 103,530,000 |\\n\\n\\\\*estimated using 4 months of original schedule, 8 months of newly proposed schedule.\\n  \\n### Specification\\n\\nAdjust the implementation of DefaultEmissionManager.sol to follow the current DAOvalidator reward schedule, gradually reducing the reward emission via subsequent AIPs until June 2025. In July 2025,  commence the Aragon 2.0 schedule of 1% annual ANT emission for validator rewards.\\n\\n### Backward Compatibility\\n\\nThis change causes no identifiable backward incompatibilities.\\n  \\n### References \\n\\n-   [Original Distribution of Staking Token Rewards](https:forum.aragon.technology/t/an-update-on-distribution-of-staking-token-rewards/9654/)\\n    \\n### Copyright\\n\\nAll copyrights and related rights in this work are waived under CC0 1.0 Universal.\\n\\n\\n", "resourcesJson": "[{\\"name\\":\\"Github\\",\\"url\\":\\"https://github.com/xavikh/fantastic-aip/blob/main/AIPs/AIP-26.md\\"}]"}

{"title": "Adjust ANT Total Supply", "summary": "Proposes burning ANT tokens corresponding to previously burned DAO", "description": "## Abstract\\n\\nIn response to community feedback, this AIP proposes that the `burn()` function be called on the migrator contract, introduced in [AIP-17](https://github.com/xavikh/fantastic-aip/blob/main/AIPs/AIP-17.md), in order to burn an amount of ANT equivalent to DAOin the addresses currently used to permanently remove DAOtokens from supply (ex: 0x000..dEaD) as well as other permanently inaccessible tokens, thus bringing the ANT circulating supply inline with the DAOsupply.\\n\\n### Motivation\\n\\n[AIP-17](https://github.com/xavikh/fantastic-aip/blob/main/AIPs/AIP-17.md), which initiates the ANT upgrade, proposes the minting of 10B ANT to the migrator contract which corresponds to the entire original supply of DAO. In order to accurately reflect the amount of previously burned DAOin the ANT supply, the authors propose the burning of corresponding ANT.\\n\\n### Specification\\n\\nCall `burn()` on the migrator contract with an amount reflective of the DAObalances of the following addresses:\\n\\n-   0x000000000000000000000000000000000000dEaD \\\\[DAOburn address\\\\] - 28,048,923.20944512682731682 DAO\\n-   0x7D1AfA7B718fb893dB30A3aBc0Cfc608AaCfeBB0 \\\\[DAOtoken address\\\\] - 479,955.33752218388182171 DAO\\n\\n*Exact values determined by the [Agra Hardfork](https://github.com/xavikh/fantastic-aip/blob/main/AIPs/AIP-28.md)*\\n\\n### Backward Compatibility\\n\\nThis change causes no identifiable backward incompatibilities. \\n\\n### Copyright\\n\\nAll copyrights and related rights in this work are waived under CC0 1.0 Universal.\\n", "resourcesJson": "[{\\"name\\":\\"Forum\\",\\"url\\":\\"https://forum.aragon.technology/t/aip-25-adjust-ant-total-supply/13008\\"},{\\"name\\":\\"Github\\",\\"url\\":\\"https://github.com/xavikh/fantastic-aip/blob/main/AIPs/AIP-25.md\\"}]"}

{"title": "Ancient data pruning", "summary": "Ability to prune ancient blockchain data for PoS", "description": "## Abstract\\n\\nA full node of the primary client of Aragon PoS, [bor](https://github.com/test/bor) bifurcates data storage to the following areas:\\n1. LevelDB (A key-value store)\\n2. Freezer Database\\n\\nThe most recent data (the past `90000` blocks (a configurable limit)) is stored in LevelDB for faster read/write on the most recent block data and state. The data beyond this limit is moved to the Freezer Database, which stores the historical data. For a full node, the Freezer Database stores block headers, body and receipts for fulfilling RPC requests over them in a flattened raw binary format. Currently, as bor is derived from geth, it supports state pruning, which prunes the old state present in the key-value store, accumulating as the chain progresses. This reduces disk space as we remove the state data that is not required.\\n\\n### Motivation\\n\\nCurrently, the disk size of the bor database on mainnet and testnet are as follows:\\n\\n1.  Mainnet:\\n\\n* Total datadir: 5 TB\\n* Ancient dir: 1.7 TB\\n\\n2.  Testnet:\\n\\n* Total datadir: 461 GB\\n* Ancient dir: 191 GB\\n\\nThe ancient data accounts for 34% and 41% of the total size in mainnet and testnet respectively.\\n\\nWhile this data is required for nodes to serve RPC requests on historical blocks, it is not a necessity for a node to store this data if its purpose is to produce/validate a block and participate in consensus (i.e. a validator node) or a regular sentry node. Currently, there's no way to prune the Freezer/Ancient database from a full node.\\n\\nHaving a way to prune this data helps in reducing the disk space for normal full nodes that don't serve historical RPC requests. Moreover, it would enable a step towards enabling [EIP 4444](https://eips.ethereum.org/EIPS/eip-4444) for Aragon PoS (although it's a bit different, it's a step in the same direction). This AIP proposes the bor client to have the ability to prune ancient data reducing the overall disk space consumption.\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n### Specification\\n\\nThis AIP proposes to introduce a new cli command for pruning the ancient data as follows:\\n\\n```\\nUsage: bor snapshot prune-block [options...]\\n  This command will prune ancient blockchain data at the given datadir location. Options:\\n  -datadir <value>                  Path of the data directory to store information\\n  -datadir.ancient <value>          Path of the old ancient data directory\\n  -block-amount-reserved <value>    Sets the expected reserved number of blocks for offline block prune (default: 1024)\\n  -cache <value>                    Megabytes of memory allocated to internal caching (default: 1024)\\n  -cache.triesinmemory              Number of block states (tries) to keep in memory (default: 128)\\n  -check-snapshot-with-mpt          Enable checking between snapshot and Merkle Patricia Tree (default: false)\\n\\n``` \\n\\nThis command should be used in an _offline_ mode.\\n\\n### Rationale\\n\\nThe prune command keeps the last `N` blocks in the Freezer database and removes the rest. Value of `N` can vary from 0 to `K`, where `K` is the length of the chain excluding the blocks in LevelDB (90k default) and can be set using the flag `block-amount-reserved`. Basically, the pruning process will begin from genesis moving towards the most recent block. Due to the nature of implementation, pruning can be done multiple times.\\n\\nFor example: the Freezer database has these blocks: \\\\[0, 1, ..., 999, 1000\\\\]. If the value of `block-amount-reserved` is set to 100, then \\\\[0, 1, ..., 900\\\\] will be pruned and the database will be left with \\\\[901, ..., 1000\\\\]. On the second round of pruning with the value of `block-amount-reserved` set to 50, the remaining values will be \\\\[951, ..., 1000\\\\].\\n\\nThe pruner maintains an `offset` in the key value store, which depicts the start block number of the Freezer db containing ancient data. The pruner keeps updating this value after each pruning round and uses it for the next round to determine the starting point. The pruner opens a backup Freezer db and moves the blocks to be kept from the old db location. Upon completion, it performs the validation to make sure the Freezer db and kvDB (LevelDB) are in continuity and proceeds to delete the old db fully.\\n\\nAlthough the implementation is straightforward, it opens up some security concerns which are addressed in the security considerations section below.\\n\\n### Backwards Compatibility\\n\\nAlthough this feature doesn't need a hard fork, the changes are backwards incompatible (unless a separate fork is maintained). If pruning is done (at least once), the `offset` value is updated and used to open the Freezer db. Moreover, it helps in maintaining context that the old historical block data isn't available. The versions of bor which don't support pruning will fail to open the Freezer db as it doesn't have the required ancient data and won't know the `offset` value.\\n\\n\\n### Security Considerations\\n\\nThis section mentions some concerns raised by this change and also some alternatives to achieve the same. \\n\\n1.  Impacts the data availability of the chain as nodes maintaining/serving that data will be reduced.\\n-   It will be difficult to find peers who will have (and hence serve) historical data in the p2p network (a variation of EIP 4444).   \\n-   A simple counterargument for this concern is that node operators can rely heavily on snapshots for initial synchronization instead of syncing from scratch. \\n-   For querying historical data via RPC, people will move to RPC providers who are incentivized to serve and keep that data.\\n    \\n2.  Disk costs can be reduced/mitigated by moving Freezer db containing historical data to a cheaper storage medium like HDD with a simple configuration change, removing the need for this feature.\\n\\n### Copyright\\n\\nAll copyrights and related rights in this work are waived under [CCO 1.0 Universal](https://creativecommons.org/publicdomain/zero/1.0/legalcode).\\n", "resourcesJson": "[{\\"name\\":\\"Forum\\",\\"url\\":\\"https://forum.aragon.technology/t/aip-32-ancient-data-pruning/13346\\"},{\\"name\\":\\"Github\\",\\"url\\":\\"https://github.com/xavikh/fantastic-aip/blob/main/AIPs/AIP-32.md\\"}]"}
