{"title": "[PRC #2] Activate PWN v1.3 contracts", "summary": "Set tags in PWNHub for PWN v1.3 contracts to enable their use in the protocol.", "description": "

This proposal aims to activate PWN protocol version 1.3 by setting specific tags on PWNHub for the newly deployed contracts. The update will introduce new features and changes while maintaining backward compatibility with the previous version (v1.2).

Contract Updates

The following contracts will be tagged with ACTIVE_LOAN, LOAN_PROPOSAL, and/or NONCE_MANAGER on PWNHub:

  • PWNSimpleLoan v1.3

  • PWNSimpleLoanSimpleProposal v1.3

  • PWNSimpleLoanListProposal v1.3

  • PWNSimpleLoanElasticProposal v1.1

  • PWNSimpleLoanDutchAuctionProposal v1.1

  • PWNUtilizedCredit

New Features

  • Flexible Loan Duration: Proposals can now define loan duration or specify a default date, regardless of when the proposal is accepted.

  • Shared Utilized Credit: Multiple proposals can share accepted credit limits, allowing for more efficient credit utilization.

Changes and Fixes

  • The “fungible proposal” has been renamed to “elastic proposal” for clarity.

  • Permit support has been removed from the protocol.

  • ERC712 typehash for proposals has been fixed to ensure proper functionality.

It’s important to note that the older version (v1.2) of the PWN protocol will remain active until a separate proposal is made to disable it. This approach ensures a smooth transition and allows users to adapt to the new features gradually.

", "resourcesJson": "[{\\"url\\":\\"https://forum.pwn.xyz/t/prc-2-activate-pwn-v1-3-contracts/60\\",\\"name\\":\\"PRC #2 forum post\\"}]"}

{"title": "[PRC #5] Setup PWN Chainlink support", "summary": "Set tags in PWN Hub for PWN Elastic Chainlink Proposal to enable its use in the protocol and accept ownership of the Chainlink Feed Registry.", "description": "

This proposal aims to activate the PWN Elastic Chainlink Proposal and transfer ownership of Chainlink Feed Registry to Protocol timelock. PWN Elastic Chainlink Proposal allows proposal signers to use Chainlink feeds in proposals and set an LTV instead of a credit-to-collateral ratio, increasing overall UX. The Chainlink feeds need to be registered in the new Chainlink Feed Registry deployment managed by PWN.

New features:

  • Proposal LTV: Proposals can set loan-to-value that will define the amount of credit and collateral on proposal acceptance. Accepting such a proposal at different times can change the credit-to-collateral ratio based on asset volatility.

", "resourcesJson": "[{\\"url\\":\\"https://forum.pwn.xyz/t/prc-5-setup-pwn-chainlink-support/69\\",\\"name\\":\\"PRC #5 forum post\\"}]"}

{"title": "Starter proposal", "summary": "This proposal aims to establish a decentralized organization (DAO) to raise funds for disaster relief through blockchain technology. The DAO will use multisig governance for secure fund management and launch an online NFT auction on Polygon to raise funds globally and use all the money raised to help people in need also bring forth revival. Everyone who contributes by buying token can vote on how we spend the money. To cover setup costs, including deploying the DAO and minting the first NFT 0.2 ETH will be funded. ", "description": "

This DAO will focus on helping communities affected by disasters by raising money through NFTs—digital items that can be bought and sold securely on the blockchain.

Governance Structure

  1. Multisig Wallet:

    • Initially, the DAO will use a multisig wallet with 3 or 5 signatories (odd number) to ensure decisions are secure and collaborative.

    • Multisig governance prevents single points of failure and ensures accountability, as multiple signers must approve transactions.

  2. Token Governance Transition:

    • After the NFT auction, a second DAO will be created on Polygon to issue governance tokens.

    • Token holders will vote on how funds are allocated using percentage-based voting (e.g., 20% of votes for Option 1 means 20% of funds allocated there).

Fundraising Process

  1. NFT Auction:

    • Inspired by UkraineDAO, we will create one meaningful NFT tied to a disaster relief cause (e.g., artwork representing affected communities).

    • The NFT will be minted on Polygon for low-cost transactions and auctioned globally for 32 hours to accommodate donors across time zones.

  2. Fund Distribution:

    • Funds raised through the NFT auction will be held in the multisig wallet.

    • Token holders will vote on allocation percentages for specific purposes (e.g., food aid, medical supplies).

Why 0.2 ETH Is Sufficient

The requested amount is enough to:

  • Deploy the DAO on Ethereum Layer 1 for governance.

  • Mint the NFT on Polygon Layer 2 for fundraising.

  • Cover gas fees for transactions and setup.

Breakdown of Costs:

  • Deploying DAO: 0.05 ETH

  • Minting NFT: 0.05 ETH

  • Marketing and outreach: 0.05 ETH

  • Operational reserve: 0.05 ETH

Technical Specification

  1. Blockchain:

    • Ethereum Layer 1 for DAO governance (ensures security and decentralization).

    • Polygon Layer 2 for minting NFTs (low-cost transactions).

  2. Multisig Wallet Setup:

    • Gnosis Safe with initial configuration requiring approval from 3 or 5 signatories.

  3. NFT Auction Platform:

    • OpenSea or PartyBid for fractionalized bidding.

  4. Voting Mechanism:

    • Percentage-based fund allocation using governance tokens issued after NFT auction.

Metrics or Key Performance Indicators

We will measure success through:

  1. Deploying the DAO within 30 days.

  2. Minting and auctioning the first NFT within 45 days.

  3. Raising at least 1 ETH from the first NFT auction.

  4. Distributing funds raised within 72 hours of identifying a disaster relief need.

  5. Engaging at least 100 members in governance discussions within three months.

 

Team Description

The team currently consists of one founder with experience in humanitarian work and blockchain technology. Additional roles will include:

  1. Multisig signatories from trusted partners globally (to ensure fair governance).

  2. Volunteer artists contributing to NFT creation.

  3. Blockchain developers assisting with technical setup.

Next Steps

  1. Deploy the DAO on Ethereum Layer 1 (Week 1).

  2. Mint the first NFT on Polygon (Week 2).

  3. Launch an NFT auction on OpenSea or PartyBid (Week 3).

  4. Onboard multisig signatories from trusted partners globally (Weeks 2–4).

  5. Distribute funds raised based on token-holder voting results (Week 4).

  6. Begin outreach efforts to onboard more community members into the DAO ecosystem (Ongoing).

{"title": "Move Security Council threshold from 5/7 to 4/7", "summary": "This proposal will lower the necessary signers to push an emergency proposal.", "description": "

OSx vs OZ Governor

Both solutions aim to facilitate on‐chain governance, but they take very different design approaches:

Governance Primitive:

Aragon OSx flips the conventional model by making permissions itself the core primitive. In OSx, governance is fundamentally about precisely configuring which addresses can call which functions, thereby shaping the organization’s control over its resources.

OpenZeppelin Governor is built around token‐weighted voting. Here, the core idea is that voting power is determined by token holdings (often via ERC20Votes or similar extensions), and proposals pass based on the aggregated votes of token holders.

Modularity and Customization:

Aragon OSx embraces a plugin architecture. DAOs built with OSx can add, remove, or upgrade plugins (e.g., TokenVoting, Multisig, Admin, or even custom plugins) to tailor their governance processes without needing to fork or rewrite core functionality.

OpenZeppelin Governor is also modular but in a different way: it provides a suite of composable modules (for vote counting, quorum calculation, timelock integration, etc.) that you can mix and match by specialized developers.

However, its no-code customization is mainly focused on adjusting governance parameters (like voting delay, voting period, quorum thresholds) rather than reconfiguring the underlying permission structure.

Execution and Control:

In Aragon OSx, the DAO contract itself holds multiple potential uses (asset management, execution, permission management) and its design is centered on fine-grained access control. This allows for flexible and dynamic delegation of governance authority, where the very notion of “who can do what” is built into the core design.

In OpenZeppelin Governor, once a proposal passes through the token-voting process, execution is handled in a more linear fashion via a timelock mechanism that delays execution for security, but doesn’t fundamentally change who can execute actions.

While OpenZeppelin Governor is optimized for straightforward, token-based decision-making with strong compatibility and community support, Aragon OSx is engineered as an entire operating system for DAOs.

Permissions, plugins, and modular organizational structures take center stage, offering a radically different approach to decentralized governance.

If what you need is a straigthforward DAO that only makes decisions based on referendums, Governor is sufficient to achieve these goals.

OSx represents a better alternative for organizations that want to leverage its permission system to enable more advanced processes like optimistic governance, multiple decision flows, or including different governing bodies.

{"title": "NODEIFI DAO CONSTITUTION", "summary": "Preamble\\n\\nWe, the members of NODEIFI DAO, establish this Constitution to define our mission, values, and governance structure. Our purpose is to foster a decentralized, transparent, and community-driven ecosystem that aligns with the principles of Web3. Through collective decision-making and shared responsibility, we strive to build a sustainable and resilient DAO that serves its members and advances its mission.", "description": "

Article I: Mission & Values

Section 1.1: Mission

NODEIFI DAO exists to:

  • Enable decentralized governance and decision-making.

  • Support and fund ecosystem growth through strategic partnerships and treasury management.

  • Foster innovation by providing infrastructure and resources for Web3 projects.

  • Promote transparency, security, and efficiency in all DAO operations.

Section 1.2: Core Values

Our community is committed to the following values:

  • Decentralization: Power is distributed among members, ensuring collective governance.

  • Transparency: All governance decisions and treasury allocations are publicly auditable.

  • Inclusion: All members have an opportunity to contribute and participate.

  • Security & Integrity: We adhere to best practices in governance, finance, and technology.

Article II: DAO Membership

Section 2.1: Membership Eligibility

Membership in NODEIFI DAO is open to individuals or entities that:

  • Meet the criteria established in the Membership Bylaws.

  • Agree to uphold the principles and governance rules outlined in this Constitution.

  • Participate in governance activities, including voting and community discussions.

Section 2.2: Membership Rights

Members have the right to:

  • Submit and vote on proposals.

  • Access DAO financial reports and governance records.

  • Participate in working groups, sub-DAOs, and governance discussions.

  • Receive rewards or incentives based on their contributions.

Section 2.3: Membership Responsibilities

Members are expected to:

  • Act in the best interest of NODEIFI DAO and its community.

  • Follow governance processes and respect DAO decisions.

  • Maintain security over their access credentials and uphold privacy where applicable.

Article III: Governance Structure

Section 3.1: Governance Model

NODEIFI DAO is governed through a hybrid system that includes:

  • Direct On-Chain Governance: Major decisions are made through smart contract-based voting.

  • Executive Team: Responsible for operational oversight and executing approved decisions.

  • Working Groups & Sub-DAOs: Delegated teams focused on specific initiatives.

Section 3.2: Voting & Proposal Process

  • Any member can submit a proposal following the guidelines in the Proposal Submission & Voting Bylaws.

  • Proposals are categorized based on impact (e.g., treasury, governance, operations) and require different quorum and approval thresholds.

  • Voting is conducted through the DAO’s governance platform, with results recorded on-chain where applicable.

Section 3.3: Amendments to the Constitution

  • Amendments require a supermajority (e.g., ⅔) approval from voting members.

  • Proposals to amend the Constitution must undergo a mandatory discussion period before voting.

Article IV: Treasury & Resource Management

Section 4.1: Treasury Structure

  • NODEIFI DAO manages its treasury through multi-signature wallets, controlled by elected stewards or smart contract mechanisms.

  • Treasury funds must be allocated according to approved DAO proposals.

Section 4.2: Financial Transparency

  • Regular financial reports will be published for community review.

  • Fund movements must be trackable on-chain to maintain trust and accountability.

Article V: Dispute Resolution & Enforcement

Section 5.1: Conflict Resolution

  • Disputes between members or regarding governance issues will first be addressed through mediation.

  • If mediation fails, disputes may be escalated to a governance vote or an elected arbitration committee.

Section 5.2: Code of Conduct Violations

  • Members found in violation of governance rules may face penalties, including temporary suspension or removal, as outlined in the Bylaws.

  • Enforcement mechanisms will be handled transparently, with members having the right to appeal decisions.

Article VI: Dissolution

Section 6.1: Conditions for Dissolution

  • NODEIFI DAO may dissolve if approved by a supermajority vote.

  • Upon dissolution, remaining treasury funds shall be allocated based on a final community vote or to a pre-designated recipient.

Ratification & Adoption

This Constitution is hereby adopted and ratified by NODEIFI DAO members as the guiding framework for our governance and operations.

Signed & Approved by NODEIFI DAO Date: 5th May 2025 Version: 1.0

{"title": "Proposal Submission & Voting Guidelines", "summary": "Purpose & Scope\\nThe purpose of this document is to establish a standardized process for submitting, reviewing, and voting on proposals within NODEIFI DAO. These guidelines ensure transparency, efficiency, and inclusivity in the DAO's governance process.\\n", "description": "

Proposal Submission Process

1. Eligibility to Submit Proposals

  • Only recognized DAO members may submit proposals.

  • Proposals must align with the DAO’s mission and bylaws.

  • Members must follow the proposal format outlined below.

2. Proposal Format

Each proposal must include the following sections:

  1. Title: Clear and concise name of the proposal.

  2. Proposer(s): Name(s) or wallet address(es) of the individual(s) submitting the proposal.

  3. Summary: Brief overview of the proposal’s intent and expected outcomes.

  4. Rationale: Explanation of why the proposal is necessary and how it aligns with DAO objectives.

  5. Implementation Plan: Step-by-step plan for executing the proposal, including responsible parties and timelines.

  6. Budget & Resources: Breakdown of financial or resource requirements (if applicable).

  7. Voting Options: Clearly defined options for voting (e.g., Yes, No, Abstain).

3. Proposal Submission & Review Process

  1. Drafting & Discussion

    1. Members are encouraged to discuss potential proposals in governance forums before formal submission. (this will eventually be on Discourse) 

    2. Feedback from the community should be incorporated before finalizing the draft. (for Sub-DAO’s)

  2. Submission

    1. Proposals must be submitted through the designated DAO governance platform.

    2. A unique proposal ID will be assigned for tracking.

  3. Initial Review & Validation

    1. The Governance & Finance Lead (or designated reviewers) will check for completeness and compliance with DAO bylaws.

    2. If revisions are required, the proposer will be notified and given time to make necessary changes.

Voting Guidelines

1. Voting Eligibility

  1. Only DAO members with governance privileges (e.g., wallet holders, NFT-based voting members) can vote.

  2. Voting power is determined by the DAO’s governance model (e.g., token-weighted, one-member-one-vote, etc.).

2. Voting Process

  • Proposal Announcement

    1. Approved proposals are posted in governance channels for member review. (Discourse)

    2. Proposals should be reviewed and comments added during a specific period of time, say 5 days after which the draft is finalized and pushed to the DAO platform for voting. 

    3. A defined voting period (e.g., 3-7 days) is set before voting ends.

  • Voting Methods

    1. Voting will be conducted on the designated governance platform (e.g., Snapshot, Aragon, on-chain voting systems).

    2. Voting options include: (limited options in current platform)

      1. Yes – In favor of the proposal

      2. No – Against the proposal

      3. Abstain – No opinion or neutral stance

  • Quorum Requirements

    1. A minimum threshold of voter participation (quorum) is required for a vote to be valid.

    2. The quorum percentage will be defined by the DAO governance framework.

  • Decision Threshold

    1. A simple majority (51%) is required for most proposals.

    2. Higher thresholds (e.g., 66% or unanimous approval) may be required for critical governance changes or treasury allocations.

  • Finalization & Implementation

    1. Passed proposals will be recorded on the governance platform.

    2. The responsible teams will execute approved proposals in accordance with the implementation plan.

    3. Progress updates must be shared with the community.


Amendments & Updates

  1. This document will be reviewed periodically to ensure effectiveness.

  2. Amendments must be approved through a formal DAO governance vote.

By following these guidelines, NODEIFI DAO ensures a fair and structured decision-making process that empowers its members and maintains governance integrity.

{"title": "Upgrade OSx to 1.4 across all chains", "summary": "This proposal will update OSx, Aragon's governance smart contract framework, across all chains.", "description": "

Aragon OSx was built under the belief that: the hyperstructure for onchain governance will be a permission management system.

Permissions as a governance primitive

Safe’s governance primitive is a multisig. Governor’s governance primitive is token-weighted voting. Hats’ primitive is roles. These all use permissioning in some form, but in all of them, it’s secondary.

Aragon OSx flipped this on its head by making permissions themselves the primitive.

Permissions are simple:

  • Who: They delimit who can execute actions. Permissions can be granted to or revoked from any Ethereum address.

  • Where: Permissions represent a function in an OSx smart contract. This is the address of the contract where the function lives.

Therefore, the design of an onchain organization is the process of defining the permission configuration that results in effective management of their resources and assets.

Core contracts

While permissions can be granted to any address, the rest of the OSx contracts are aware of and make the permission manager even more powerful.

The DAO

The DAO is the main body and identity of an onchain organization. It contains all of the core functionality:

  • Arbitrary execution: The DAO address is the address that governance proposals pass actions to in order to execute. Any plugin with permission to do so can pass an array of one or more actions.

  • Asset management: It’s also the address where assets are sent and withdrawn from, acting as the central vault of the DAO.

  • Permission management: It’s also where the actual permission management logic is, such as the functions to grant and revoke permissions.

  • Utilities: It also has various supporting functionality, such as upgradeability, signature validation, and callback handling.

Plugins

Governance logic is encapsulated in reusable contracts called plugins.

Plugins are granted specific permissions depending what you want them to do. In practice, they often act as governing bodies or decision-making processes. For example, a token voting plugin or multisig plugin define all of their unique governance logic within the plugin itself. This “separation of concerns” keeps the protocol focused and ultimately more flexible.

The core plugins currently supported by Aragon are:

  • TokenVoting: Proposals are created for token holders and delegates to vote on proposals, with execution decided using a simple majority rule.

  • Multisig: Proposals are created for designated addresses to approve, with execution decided by whether a minimum number of approvers is met.

  • Admin: Proposals are created by designated addresses and immediately executed.

  • StagedProposalProcessor: Proposals are created and progress through any number of stages, with other plugins either voting to approve or veto the proposal. The SPP plugin is essentially a cross-plugin state manager that enables governance processes with multiple stages and multiple governing bodies.

  • In addition to our core plugins, new plugins are being developed to take governance on OSx even further. They can be seen in the Governance Marketplace.

Modular governance

For governance to be successful when taking place onchain, it must be modular, allowing organizations to tailor governance structures to their unique goals, risks, communities, stakeholders, and internal cultures.

OSx enables organizations to install or uninstall any number of new plugins at any time, with no limits to which permissions are granted to which plugins. The modular interplay of plugins address one of the biggest themes that we see at Aragon:

One-size-fits-all governance doesn't work.

Key Benefits - what Aragon App can uniquely provide:

  • Governing Bodies: Allows DAOs to define separate stakeholder groups, each functioning as a distinct governing body with its own specific permissions.

  • Staged Proposals: Supports governance processes that features multiple stages for proposals to go through, each governed by different bodies—all transparently displayed to show how governance decisions translate into onchain actions.

  • Optimistic Governance: Allows governing bodies to take on a passive role to veto proposals only when they disagree with it.

  • Evolving Governance: Governance processes can be added, removed, or modified to iterate on the governance as the needs of the DAO evolves.

  • Modular and Extensible UI: A UI and design system that simplifies the integration of new plugins with different governance logic.

  • DAO creation and editing assistant: Builders can use no code wizards to incrementally design their DAO, and hand over permissions to the community only once it has been built and tested first.

{"title": "Ankh DAO Charter", "summary": "Ankh DAO Charter\\n\\nVersion: v1.0\\nDate: 2025-07-07", "description": "

Ankh DAO Charter

Version: v1.0

Date: 2025-07-07

Article 1: Purpose

1.1 The purpose of Ankh DAO is to manage pooled funds through a Vault structure, enabling collective deployment under approved investment or trading strategies, and to distribute results fairly and transparently among participants.

1.2 Ankh DAO shall operate in a decentralized, transparent, and equitable manner to protect the interests of its participants.

Article 2: Roles and Participants

2.1 Participants in Ankh DAO shall include:

  • Members, who participate in governance, voting, and operational oversight

  • Vault Participants, who contribute capital to the Vault and hold specific economic rights

Article 3: Governance and Vault Policy

3.1 Recognized Members shall hold one vote per wallet address.

3.2 Ankh DAO may establish and update a Vault Policy describing the conditions under which Vault Participants engage with the DAO.

3.3 Ankh DAO shall maintain transparent, auditable processes for proposals, voting, approvals, and record-keeping consistent with its commitment to decentralized and fair practices.

Article 4: Privacy Commitment

4.1 Ankh DAO shall limit required disclosures to what is strictly necessary for its operation.

4.2 Members agree to preserve confidentiality of any private off-chain discussions and internal DAO records.

Article 5: Risk Controls and Operational Principles

5.1 Ankh DAO shall maintain reasonable risk controls and oversight practices to protect Vault assets and support sound investment or trading activities.

Article 6: Amendments

6.1 Amendments to this Charter shall require approval by a qualified majority of Members.

{"title": "Ankh Vault Policy", "summary": "Ankh Vault Policy\\n\\nVersion: v1.0\\nDate: 2025-07-07", "description": "

Ankh Vault Policy

Version: v1.0
Date: 2025-07-07

1. Purpose

1.1 This Vault Policy establishes binding terms and conditions under which Vault Participants contribute, maintain, and withdraw capital from the Ankh Vault.

1.2 This Policy forms a contractual framework between Ankh DAO and each Vault Participant, in alignment with the Ankh DAO Charter.

2. Participation

2.1 A Vault Participant contributes capital to the Vault under this Policy, subject to a minimum contribution amount of 50,000 USDC.

2.2 By contributing, Vault Participants agree to delegate operational fund management to Ankh DAO governance and accept all provisions of this Policy.

2.3 Participation is subject to a lock-in period of 90 days from the date of contribution. During the lock-in, withdrawals are not permitted.

3. Contributions

3.1 Contributions shall be made in USDC and transferred to the Vault’s designated address: arb1:0xE97d8974A5cD1Bf2846C26B6e852aE48F6220461

3.2 Ankh DAO may accept or reject contributions at its discretion if capacity or risk limits are exceeded.

4. Preferred Return and Distributions

4.1 Vault Participants are entitled to a preferred return of 20% per annum on their contributed amount, calculated from the date of contribution.

4.2 Distributions beyond the preferred return may be made yearly to active Vault Participants at the sole discretion of Ankh DAO governance.

5. Withdrawals

5.1 After the lock-in period, a Vault Participant may request a withdrawal at any time, up to the amount representing their share of the Vault based on their contribution, accrued preferred return, and any additional distributions. Withdrawals must be requested through the Ankh DAO Aragon platform as set out in Article 7.

5.2 Withdrawal requests shall be processed within a reasonable period subject to Vault liquidity, with fair treatment of all Vault Participants.

6. Reporting

6.1 Ankh DAO shall deliver Vault performance reports to Vault Participants on a quarterly basis.

7. Communications and Requests

7.1 The Ankh DAO Aragon platform shall serve as the only official channel for Vault Participants to submit withdrawal requests and receive Policy notifications.

7.2 Withdrawal requests shall be reviewed and processed by a designated DAO admin in accordance with this Policy.

8. Risk Disclaimer

8.1 Vault Participants acknowledge that all investments carry risk, and the preferred return cannot be absolutely guaranteed in case of material Vault losses.

8.2 Ankh DAO will act diligently, but cannot be held liable for losses caused by market conditions or third-party failures.

9. Amendments

9.1 Amendments to this Vault Policy shall follow the framework set out in the Ankh DAO Charter, with Vault Participants notified through the Aragon platform.

{"title": "Ratification of Team DAO Governance Framework", "summary": "This proposal ratifies the Team DAO governance framework within REIT DAO, recognizing the Contribution Policy, REPU01 Framework, Contribution Register, and evaluation procedures as the official operational rules.", "description": "

This proposal ratifies the governance framework governing the operation of Team DAO within the REIT DAO structure.

The framework consists of the following previously adopted documents and procedures:

- REIT DAO Contribution & Governance Policy

- REPU01 Governance Framework

- Contribution Register (Contributor activity record)

- AI Contribution Evaluation Prompt


Under this resolution, the above documents and procedures are recognized as the official governance and operational framework of Team DAO within REIT DAO.

Additionally, upon the admission of a new participant to Team DAO, the participant shall receive 1 REPU01 token as an initial governance token reflecting membership within Team DAO.

Related Main DAO Governance Decisions

The operation of Team DAO is also supported by the following Main DAO governance decisions:

- Approval of Operational DAO Administrators

- Approval of REPU01 Token Issuance and Allocation to Team DAO


These governance decisions establish the administrative structure and token allocation mechanisms supporting the operation of Team DAO.

{"title": "Delegación a Fundación Futuro", "summary": "Se propone delegar a Fundación Futuro la ejecución en la plataforma de las decisiones previamente aprobadas por la comunidad de Guayabillas, mediante procesos participativos offchain. Esto asegura transparencia, trazabilidad y permite avanzar mientras se fortalecen las capacidades digitales locales.", "description": "

La comunidad de Guayabillas ha trabajado en el fortalecimiento de su gobernanza territorial mediante procesos participativos, incluyendo talleres, reuniones y votaciones comunitarias. Como parte de este proceso, se estableció que las decisiones se tomen inicialmente fuera de la blockchain (offchain), utilizando mecanismos accesibles como WhatsApp y asambleas locales.

Objetivo:

Se aprueba que Fundación Futuro actúe como delegada para registrar en esta DAO las decisiones validadas por la comunidad de Guayabillas, tomadas mediante procesos participativos offchain. Esta delegación asegura transparencia, trazabilidad y permite avanzar mientras se fortalecen las capacidades digitales locales.

Alcance:

• Fundación Futuro será la responsable de ejecutar las decisiones comunitarias que se toman en sesiones y trasladarlas a la plataforma.

• Toda propuesta ingresada en esta DAO deberá incluir una referencia a su validación comunitaria previa (por reunión, acta, encuesta u otro medio participativo).

• Esta delegación podrá ser modificada o revocada mediante una nueva propuesta.

Justificación:

Este modelo asegura:

• Inclusión de los miembros de la DAO, más allá de las barreras tecnológicas.

• Transparencia y trazabilidad en la ejecución de decisiones.

• Evolución progresiva hacia un modelo de gobernanza más descentralizado, sin frenar la implementación de acciones urgentes.

{"title": "NODEIFI DAO Executive Team, Structure, Roles, and Responsibilities", "summary": "This document outlines the structure, roles, and responsibilities of the NODEIFI DAO Executive Team. The team consists of five core roles, each designed to manage and sustain the DAO’s operations post-setup. These roles ensure accountability, efficient decision-making, and alignment with the DAO’s long-term objectives.\\n", "description": "

Executive Team Overview

Team Lead

  1. Leadership: Acts as the primary leader of the DAO, ensuring alignment with its vision and strategic goals.

  2. Collaboration: Works closely with the Executive Team members to facilitate decision-making and ensure cohesive action.

    1. Consults the Governance & Finance Lead on policy implementation and operational transparency.

    2. Engages with the Technical Lead to align technical initiatives with strategic objectives.

    3. Supports the Partnerships & Growth Lead in identifying high-impact collaborations.

    4. Provides guidance to the Community & Operations Lead on communication strategies.

  3. Oversight: Ensures team alignment and progress toward agreed goals by working closely with individual department leads. Major decisions, including governance changes, partnerships, and funding allocations, will be determined by a team vote rather than unilateral approval.

  4. Strategic Vision: Drives the long-term vision of NODEIFI DAO and the Velocity Foundation ecosystem, ensuring the DAO remains innovative, adaptable, and well-positioned in the Web3 space. Leads initiatives to explore new technologies, market opportunities, and strategic pivots when necessary.

  5. Treasury Oversight: Accountable for the strategic management of treasury funds. Works closely with the Governance & Finance Lead to ensure adherence to treasury objectives. Ensures major financial allocations align with DAO objectives.

  6. Stakeholder Engagement: Represents NODEIFI DAO in external discussions with partners, sub-DAOs, and other stakeholders or may appoint a representative as needed.

Governance & Finance Lead

  1. Governance Refinement: Continuously improve governance processes based on community feedback and best practices. This includes establishing clear feedback mechanisms, such as regular surveys, open forums on Discord, and dedicated governance channels on the DAO platform. All community input will be evaluated and incorporated into iterative updates to governance policies, ensuring transparency and inclusivity.

  2. Proposal Oversight: Review, organize, and track proposals submitted by members.

  3. Financial Management:

    1. Oversee treasury operations, including fund allocation and management.

    2. Maintain accurate financial records (bookkeeping) for transparency and auditing purposes.

    3. Ensure compliance with DAO policies related to fund usage.

    4. Publish treasury updates for community awareness in collaboration with the Community & Operations Lead.

  4. Operational Transparency: Ensure all financial reporting and sub-DAO updates are accurate and accessible.

  5. Task Coordination: Facilitate smooth execution of governance-related tasks and operations.

  6. Risk & Compliance: Continual research in this area to keep us honest and compliant. 

Technical Lead

  1. Infrastructure Management: Oversees all technical operations, including node infrastructure, platform development, and integrations.

  2. Technology Strategy: Advises on the selection and implementation of tools to support DAO operations.

  3. Support & Maintenance: Ensures uptime and performance of all technical systems, including blockchain infrastructure.

  4. Budgeting: Works with the Governance & Finance Lead to ensure infrastructure costs are tracked and funded.

  5. Technical Documentation: Maintains and updates technical documentation for transparency and knowledge sharing. Access to this documentation will be granted to all Executive Team members via the DAO’s repository, ensuring easy reference. Public versions will be shared with the broader DAO community through designated channels, such as the governance platform and Discord, to promote transparency and inclusivity.

Partnerships & Growth Lead

  1. Partnership Development: Identifies and secures strategic partnerships to grow the DAO’s ecosystem and resources.

  2. Revenue Opportunities: Explores new revenue streams or funding opportunities to sustain DAO operations.

  3. Community Collaboration: Works with the Community & Operations Lead to align partnership opportunities with community interests.

  4. Market Research: Analyzes trends in the Web3 space to identify growth opportunities.

  5. Brand Alignment: Ensures all partnerships and growth strategies align with NODEIFI DAO’s mission and values.

  6. Content Creation:

    1. Develops and manages social media, blogs, newsletters, and other content to maintain transparency and engagement.

    2. Ensures marketing efforts align with community needs and broader operational goals.

    3. Organizes and leads AMA sessions to foster community involvement and transparency.

  7. Event Management: Organizes external events, calls, and educational sessions to promote the DAO.

  8. Feedback Collection: Actively gathers feedback from partners and the community using tools like surveys, Telegram discussions, and dedicated feedback forms on the DAO platform. Reviews and compiles this input to share with the Governance & Finance Lead for integration into governance updates and workflows.

Community & Operations Lead

  1. Community Engagement:

  2. Manages all CandyShop DAO members, including proposal submissions and community interactions.

  3. Organizes and leads community calls, events, and forums to maintain engagement.

  4. Ensures smooth onboarding for new members and contributors.

  5. Proposal Management: Oversees the submission and review of proposals from the community to ensure alignment with the DAO’s goals.

  6. Token Distribution Oversight: Manages the process of distributing tokens to members, partners, and contributors in alignment with governance policies.

  7. Operational Support: Initiates operational tasks related to community-driven initiatives, including new project rollouts and collaborations. Raise reconciliation and token distribution and manage claims through the portal. 

  8. Feedback Integration: Gathers feedback from community members and ensures it is considered in decision-making processes. Feedback will be collected through tools such as surveys, telegram discussions, and dedicated feedback forms on the DAO platform. This input will be compiled, reviewed, and shared with the Governance & Finance Lead to integrate into governance updates and decision-making workflows.

  9. Treasury Transparency: Collaborates with the Governance & Finance Lead to publish regular treasury updates and ensure the community understands financial decisions.

Collaborative Responsibilities

While each role has specific responsibilities, the Executive Team will collaborate on the following:

  • Treasury Management:

    1. Governance & Finance Lead handles day-to-day treasury activities and reporting.

    2. Team Lead provides final oversight and approves high-stakes allocations.

    3. Technical Lead ensures infrastructure costs are managed effectively.

    4. Partnerships & Growth Lead identifies opportunities to grow treasury reserves.

    5. Community & Operations Lead communicates treasury updates to the community and manages distribution of tokens via the platform as required.

  • Community Engagement and Feedback Integration:

    1. The Community & Operations Lead will lead efforts to gather feedback from community members via surveys, dedicated governance channels, and Discord discussions.

    2. Governance & Finance Lead will evaluate and integrate this feedback into policy and operational updates.

    3. Team Lead will ensure that community-driven priorities are factored into strategic decisions.

  • Sub-DAO Oversight:

    1. Governance & Finance Lead drafts and enforces operational guidelines.

    2. Team Lead ensures sub-DAOs align with the overarching goals of NODEIFI DAO.

    3. Community & Operations Lead communicates updates and performance metrics from sub-DAOs to the broader community.

  • Strategic Planning:

    1. Regular team meetings to align on milestones and strategic goals.

    2. Feedback sessions to evaluate ongoing operations and improve workflows.

    3. The Community & Operations Lead will ensure any new partners, projects, or initiatives introduced are aligned with strategic goals and adequately supported operationally.

Accountability & Dismissal

To ensure the integrity and effectiveness of the NODEIFI DAO Executive Team, all members are expected to operate in alignment with the DAO’s principles, governance policies, and ethical standards. Failure to meet these expectations may result in dismissal.

Grounds for Dismissal

A member of the Executive Team may face dismissal under the following circumstances:

  1. Breach of DAO Principles – Engaging in actions that contradict the mission, values, or governance framework of NODEIFI DAO.

  2. Lack of Participation – Repeated failure to fulfill assigned responsibilities, attend key meetings, or engage in DAO decision-making.

  3. Misconduct or Conflict of Interest – Engaging in unethical behavior, financial mismanagement, breach of NDA or actions that compromise the DAO’s reputation or security.

  4. Misuse of Treasury Funds – Unauthorized or reckless financial decisions that endanger the sustainability of the DAO.

  5. Failure to Maintain Transparency – Withholding critical information, misrepresenting data, or failing to document and communicate key decisions.

Dismissal Process

  • Issue Identification – Concerns regarding an Executive Team member’s conduct or performance may be raised by any other Executive Team member or the broader DAO community.

  • Formal Review – The issue is reviewed by the remaining Executive Team members, who assess evidence and determine whether further action is necessary.

  • Community Oversight (if applicable) – If the issue is significant, a DAO-wide vote may be initiated to determine whether dismissal is warranted.

  • Final Decision – A dismissal decision requires a majority vote among the Executive Team (excluding the individual in question). If a DAO-wide vote is required, the threshold for approval will align with existing governance parameters.

  • Transition & Replacement – If a member is dismissed, a temporary or permanent replacement will be appointed according to governance procedures.

Right to Appeal

A dismissed member may submit an appeal within a specified period (e.g., 7 days) if they believe the decision was unfair. The appeal process will involve a secondary review by the Executive Team or a community vote, depending on the severity of the case.

This framework ensures accountability within the Executive Team while maintaining fairness and adherence to decentralized governance principles.

Accountability and Documentation

This document will be published in the DAO’s official repository for reference and accountability. Regular reviews of this document will occur on a quarterly basis to ensure that roles, responsibilities, and workflows remain relevant to the evolving needs of NODEIFI DAO.

These reviews will consider changes in community dynamics, operational requirements, and the expanding scope of responsibilities for roles such as the Community & Operations Lead. Input from surveys, Discord discussions, and governance forums will be integrated into these updates to maintain alignment with the DAO’s mission.

Updates will incorporate input gathered from community feedback channels such as surveys, Discord discussions, and DAO governance platforms, ensuring the Executive Team remains aligned with the DAO's mission and values.

Updates to roles and responsibilities will be made as needed to reflect the evolving needs of NODEIFI DAO and its ecosystem.

{"title": "Proposal Submission & Voting Guidelines", "summary": "Purpose & Scope\\nThe purpose of this document is to establish a standardized process for submitting, reviewing, and voting on proposals within NODEIFI DAO. These guidelines ensure transparency, efficiency, and inclusivity in the DAO's governance process.", "description": "

1. Eligibility to Submit Proposals

  • Only recognized DAO members may submit proposals.

  • Proposals must align with the DAO’s mission and bylaws.

  • Members must follow the proposal format outlined below.

2. Proposal Format

Each proposal must include the following sections:

  1. Title: Clear and concise name of the proposal.

  2. Proposer(s): Name(s) or wallet address(es) of the individual(s) submitting the proposal.

  3. Summary: Brief overview of the proposal’s intent and expected outcomes.

  4. Rationale: Explanation of why the proposal is necessary and how it aligns with DAO objectives.

  5. Implementation Plan: Step-by-step plan for executing the proposal, including responsible parties and timelines.

  6. Budget & Resources: Breakdown of financial or resource requirements (if applicable).

  7. Voting Options: Clearly defined options for voting (e.g., Yes, No, Abstain).

3. Proposal Submission & Review Process

  1. Drafting & Discussion

    1. Members are encouraged to discuss potential proposals in governance forums before formal submission. (this will eventually be on Discourse) 

    2. Feedback from the community should be incorporated before finalizing the draft. (for Sub-DAO’s)

  2. Submission

    1. Proposals must be submitted through the designated DAO governance platform.

    2. A unique proposal ID will be assigned for tracking.

  3. Initial Review & Validation

    1. The Governance & Finance Lead (or designated reviewers) will check for completeness and compliance with DAO bylaws.

    2. If revisions are required, the proposer will be notified and given time to make necessary changes.

Voting Guidelines

1. Voting Eligibility

  1. Only DAO members with governance privileges (e.g., wallet holders, NFT-based voting members) can vote.

  2. Voting power is determined by the DAO’s governance model (e.g., token-weighted, one-member-one-vote, etc.).

2. Voting Process

  • Proposal Announcement

    1. Approved proposals are posted in governance channels for member review. (Discourse)

    2. Proposals should be reviewed and comments added during a specific period of time, say 5 days after which the draft is finalized and pushed to the DAO platform for voting. 

    3. A defined voting period (e.g., 3-7 days) is set before voting ends.

  • Voting Methods

    1. Voting will be conducted on the designated governance platform (e.g., Snapshot, Aragon, on-chain voting systems).

    2. Voting options include: (limited options in current platform)

      1. Yes – In favor of the proposal

      2. No – Against the proposal

      3. Abstain – No opinion or neutral stance

  • Quorum Requirements

    1. A minimum threshold of voter participation (quorum) is required for a vote to be valid.

    2. The quorum percentage will be defined by the DAO governance framework.

  • Decision Threshold

    1. A simple majority (51%) is required for most proposals.

    2. Higher thresholds (e.g., 66% or unanimous approval) may be required for critical governance changes or treasury allocations.

  • Finalization & Implementation

    1. Passed proposals will be recorded on the governance platform.

    2. The responsible teams will execute approved proposals in accordance with the implementation plan.

    3. Progress updates must be shared with the community.

Amendments & Updates

  1. This document will be reviewed periodically to ensure effectiveness.

  2. Amendments must be approved through a formal DAO governance vote.

By following these guidelines, NODEIFI DAO ensures a fair and structured decision-making process that empowers its members and maintains governance integrity.

{"title": "NODEIFI DAO CONSTITUTION", "summary": "Preamble\\nWe, the members of NODEIFI DAO, establish this Constitution to define our mission, values, and governance structure. Our purpose is to foster a decentralized, transparent, and community-driven ecosystem that aligns with the principles of Web3. Through collective decision-making and shared responsibility, we strive to build a sustainable and resilient DAO that serves its members and advances its mission.", "description": "

Article I: Mission & Values

Section 1.1: Mission

NODEIFI DAO exists to:

  • Enable decentralized governance and decision-making.

  • Support and fund ecosystem growth through strategic partnerships and treasury management.

  • Foster innovation by providing infrastructure and resources for Web3 projects.

  • Promote transparency, security, and efficiency in all DAO operations.

Section 1.2: Core Values

Our community is committed to the following values:

  • Decentralization: Power is distributed among members, ensuring collective governance.

  • Transparency: All governance decisions and treasury allocations are publicly auditable.

  • Inclusion: All members have an opportunity to contribute and participate.

  • Security & Integrity: We adhere to best practices in governance, finance, and technology.


Article II: DAO Membership

Section 2.1: Membership Eligibility

Membership in NODEIFI DAO is open to individuals or entities that:

  • Meet the criteria established in the Membership Bylaws.

  • Agree to uphold the principles and governance rules outlined in this Constitution.

  • Participate in governance activities, including voting and community discussions.

Section 2.2: Membership Rights

Members have the right to:

  • Submit and vote on proposals.

  • Access DAO financial reports and governance records.

  • Participate in working groups, sub-DAOs, and governance discussions.

  • Receive rewards or incentives based on their contributions.

Section 2.3: Membership Responsibilities

Members are expected to:

  • Act in the best interest of NODEIFI DAO and its community.

  • Follow governance processes and respect DAO decisions.

  • Maintain security over their access credentials and uphold privacy where applicable.

Article III: Governance Structure

Section 3.1: Governance Model

NODEIFI DAO is governed through a hybrid system that includes:

  • Direct On-Chain Governance: Major decisions are made through smart contract-based voting.

  • Executive Team: Responsible for operational oversight and executing approved decisions.

  • Working Groups & Sub-DAOs: Delegated teams focused on specific initiatives.



Section 3.2: Voting & Proposal Process

  • Any member can submit a proposal following the guidelines in the Proposal Submission & Voting Bylaws.

  • Proposals are categorized based on impact (e.g., treasury, governance, operations) and require different quorum and approval thresholds.

  • Voting is conducted through the DAO’s governance platform, with results recorded on-chain where applicable.

Section 3.3: Amendments to the Constitution

  • Amendments require a supermajority (e.g., ⅔) approval from voting members.

  • Proposals to amend the Constitution must undergo a mandatory discussion period before voting.

Article IV: Treasury & Resource Management

Section 4.1: Treasury Structure

  • NODEIFI DAO manages its treasury through multi-signature wallets, controlled by elected stewards or smart contract mechanisms.

  • Treasury funds must be allocated according to approved DAO proposals.

Section 4.2: Financial Transparency

  • Regular financial reports will be published for community review.

  • Fund movements must be trackable on-chain to maintain trust and accountability.

Article V: Dispute Resolution & Enforcement

Section 5.1: Conflict Resolution

  • Disputes between members or regarding governance issues will first be addressed through mediation.

  • If mediation fails, disputes may be escalated to a governance vote or an elected arbitration committee.


Section 5.2: Code of Conduct Violations

  • Members found in violation of governance rules may face penalties, including temporary suspension or removal, as outlined in the Bylaws.

  • Enforcement mechanisms will be handled transparently, with members having the right to appeal decisions.

Article VI: Dissolution

Section 6.1: Conditions for Dissolution

  • NODEIFI DAO may dissolve if approved by a supermajority vote.

  • Upon dissolution, remaining treasury funds shall be allocated based on a final community vote or to a pre-designated recipient.

Ratification & Adoption

This Constitution is hereby adopted and ratified by NODEIFI DAO members as the guiding framework for our governance and operations.

Signed & Approved by NODEIFI DAO Date: 5th May 2025 Version: 1.0

  2. Formal Review – The issue is reviewed by the remaining Executive Team members, who assess evidence and determine whether further action is necessary.

  3. Community Oversight (if applicable) – If the issue is significant, a DAO-wide vote may be initiated to determine whether dismissal is warranted.

  4. Final Decision – A dismissal decision requires a majority vote among the Executive Team (excluding the individual in question). If a DAO-wide vote is required, the threshold for approval will align with existing governance parameters.

  5. Transition & Replacement – If a member is dismissed, a temporary or permanent replacement will be appointed according to governance procedures.

Right to Appeal

A dismissed member may submit an appeal within a specified period (e.g., 7 days) if they believe the decision was unfair. The appeal process will involve a secondary review by the Executive Team or a community vote, depending on the severity of the case.

This framework ensures accountability within the Executive Team while maintaining fairness and adherence to decentralized governance principles.

Accountability and Documentation

This document will be published in the DAO’s official repository for reference and accountability. Regular reviews of this document will occur on a quarterly basis to ensure that roles, responsibilities, and workflows remain relevant to the evolving needs of NODEIFI DAO. These reviews will consider changes in community dynamics, operational requirements, and the expanding scope of responsibilities for roles such as the Community & Operations Lead. Input from surveys, Discord discussions, and governance forums will be integrated into these updates to maintain alignment with the DAO’s mission. Updates will incorporate input gathered from community feedback channels such as surveys, Discord discussions, and DAO governance platforms, ensuring the Executive Team remains aligned with the DAO's mission and values. Updates to roles and responsibilities will be made as needed to reflect the evolving needs of NODEIFI DAO and its ecosystem.

To qualify as a NODEIFI DAO member, individuals or entities must meet the following criteria:

  • Adhere to the governance principles and rules outlined in the DAO’s interim governance bylaws.

  • Actively participate in DAO activities, governance discussions, and voting processes.

  • Conduct themselves in alignment with the DAO’s ethical and operational standards.

Responsibilities & Expectations

As a member of NODEIFI DAO, individuals agree to:

  • Uphold the principles of decentralization, transparency, and collective decision-making.

  • Act in good faith to support the best interests of the DAO and its ecosystem.

  • Participate in governance decisions, including proposal reviews and voting.

  • Comply with all interim governance bylaws and any amendments ratified by the DAO.

  • Avoid conflicts of interest and disclose any potential conflicts when engaging in DAO matters.

Purpose & Vision

NODEIFI DAO is established to advance the Velocity Foundation ecosystem by fostering innovation, decentralization, and strategic growth. The DAO exists to provide governance, treasury oversight, and community-driven decision-making that aligns with its long-term objectives.

Decision-Making Process

  • Voting mechanisms will be determined based on membership structure (initially wallet-based, with future potential for NFTs or tokens).

  • A proposal is considered approved upon meeting the required quorum and majority threshold.


Treasury Oversight

  • Treasury management will be conducted with transparency and strategic intent.

  • Major financial allocations must be reviewed by the Executive Team and ratified via DAO vote.

Disciplinary & Dismissal Procedures

  • Members or Executive Team members found in violation of governance principles may face disciplinary action, including warnings, suspension, or removal.

  • Any disciplinary action must follow a structured review process to ensure fairness and adherence to governance standards.

Future Transition Plan

  • As NODEIFI DAO evolves, governance mechanisms may shift towards token-based or NFT-based models.

  • Bylaws will be reviewed periodically to reflect changes in DAO structure, governance requirements, and ecosystem growth.

By participating in NODEIFI DAO, members agree to abide by these bylaws and contribute to the sustainable growth of the DAO.

{"title": "Add \\"The Oops Button\\" to the Kill Switch Naming Pool", "summary": "Description:\\nShould HALP include “The Oops Button” as an official kill switch term?\\nThis friendly name balances approachability with mild panic and has excellent UI appeal.\\n\\nOptions:\\n✅ Yes – Add it to the naming pool\\n❌ No – Exclude it from official use\\n⚪ Abstain", "description": "

In our ongoing effort to establish a robust and slightly ridiculous AI safety protocol, HALP must standardise the terminology used in emergency shutdown systems — otherwise known as \\"kill switches.\\"

This proposal seeks to formally include \\"The Oops Button\\" in HALP’s official naming pool for such systems.

Rationale:
\\"The Oops Button\\" strikes the perfect balance between disarming charm and existential dread. It conveys the seriousness of the situation with a hint of levity — reminding us that, yes, something has gone very wrong... but at least we’re handling it with manners.

Adopting this term would:

  • Encourage user-friendly UI design for critical systems

  • Set a tone of accountable absurdity, in keeping with HALP's broader philosophy

  • Make documentation, incident reports, and post-mortems more tolerable to read

If approved, “The Oops Button” will become an officially sanctioned term within HALP’s lexicon and may be used in proposals, dashboards, technical documents, and onboarding guides.

Question to Vote On:

Should HALP include \\"The Oops Button\\" as an official kill switch term?

  • ✅ Yes – Add it to the naming pool

  • ❌ No – Exclude it from official use

  • ⚪ Abstain – I have no strong feelings, but I respect the button

{"title": "Authorization to Investigate and Implement a $TRAXA Token Launch Strategy in Support of Traxa DAO’s Mission", "summary": "This proposal seeks DAO Council approval to develop a $TRAXA token launch strategy over 60–90 days. The goal is to activate incentives, re-engage contributors, and prepare for sustainable growth. No token will be launched without further DAO approval. The strategy will include tokenomics, legal review, contributor rewards, and a phased roadmap. Council retains full control over final implementation.", "description": "

This proposal seeks DAO Council approval to investigate and develop a comprehensive $TRAXA token launch strategy. The initiative is designed to activate the DAO’s incentive structure, re-engage contributors, and establish a framework for sustainable ecosystem growth.

This authorization is limited to research and strategic planning. DAO Council members will retain full authority over implementation approval via a subsequent proposal. No token deployment or distribution will proceed without that formal approval.

Background and Foundation:

Traxa’s public inception began with the registration of Traxa.io on May 24, 2021, establishing its earliest public footprint. This was followed by key formal milestones:

  • October 14, 2021: Formation of Traxa DAO LLC as a legally recognized DAO entity in Wyoming.

  • October 15, 2021: Execution of a SAFT agreement with a strategic ecosystem partner.

  • October 18, 2021: Issuance of the DAO’s EIN by the IRS.

Since then, Traxa has developed prototype technology, engaged in public-sector pilot opportunities, and demonstrated a working model for decentralized supply chain data visibility. What remains is the structured activation of a token economy to scale participation, governance, and infrastructure growth.

Why Now:

1. Technological Readiness

  • Functional prototype validated through live use.

  • Architecture supports decentralized, incentivized data capture.

2. Contributor Network

  • High-quality contributors are interested in re-engagement if a clear compensation model exists.

  • The token is the missing link to align incentives and commitment.

3. Regulatory Climate

  • U.S. digital asset policy is more navigable than in 2021.

  • Traxa DAO LLC, operating under a Wyoming DAO statute with a previous SAFT agreement, is well-positioned to proceed responsibly.

4. Market Timing

  • Global supply chain disruptions and demand for visibility continue.

  • The rise of real-world asset (RWA) tokenization and decentralized infrastructure networks (DePIN) puts Traxa at the intersection of two accelerating trends.

Scope of Authorization:

This proposal authorizes a working group to prepare a $TRAXA token launch strategy, including:

  1. Tokenomics and Strategy Design

    • Drafting a model that supports utility, participation, and DAO alignment.

  2. Compliance and Legal Evaluation

    • Ensuring regulatory alignment across U.S. and international frameworks.

  3. Contributor Incentive Plan

    • Designing a fair and secure rewards system for prior and future contributors.

  4. Roadmap and Ecosystem Research

    • Evaluating deployment options and outlining a phased roadmap.

Deliverables (Due in 60–90 Days):

  • Comprehensive tokenomics plan

  • Legal/regulatory summary

  • Contributor compensation and engagement framework

  • Implementation roadmap with ecosystem alignment

DAO Council members will retain full authority over implementation approval via a subsequent proposal.

Safeguards:

  • No tokens will be launched, sold, or distributed under this authorization.

  • Sensitive launch parameters (e.g., timing, allocations) will remain confidential during the planning phase.

  • All outputs will be reviewed transparently by the DAO Council prior to implementation.

Voting Options:

  • ✅ YES — Approve a 60–90 day planning phase to develop a $TRAXA token strategy. Final implementation will require separate DAO Council approval.

  • ❌ NO — Decline to pursue a token strategy at this time.

Conclusion:

Traxa DAO was founded in 2021 with a vision of transforming global supply chain transparency. With its technological foundation in place and growing alignment between contributor interest, regulatory viability, and market timing, the DAO is ready to explore the next phase.

This proposal ensures the DAO moves deliberately, strategically, and in full control of the path forward.

💡 Summary

Transfer 1 083.2467 DAI from the Traxa DAO vault to wallet 0x49d33037Fb37bd8f4A4817BF425540c2D5E4d30E. That wallet will swap the full amount on Uniswap for POL (Polygon 2.0 base token), then immediately send all received POL to the DAO Treasury address 0xf8b18BBa2c235b887B79F1f3AC134De19FaB8924. Converting idle DAI to POL positions our treasury on the native network where upcoming grants, pilots, and incentives are paid.

🔍 Background

  • Assets now sit in the Aragon vault on Polygon.

  • Our grant applications (Polygon Ecosystem, QuickSwap Growth Fund, etc.) and DePIN pilots pay out in POL/MATIC.

  • Polygon is migrating from MATIC to POL; holding POL ensures liquidity for fees, staking, and governance.

💰 Request Details

StepToken / AmountFromToPurpose11 083.2467 DAIDAO Vault0x49d3…d30EFund swap wallet2Swap 100 % of DAI → POL (Uniswap v3, max 0.5 % slippage)0x49d3…d30E0x49d3…d30EAcquire POL3All received POL0x49d3…d30EDAO Treasury 0xf8b1…8924Consolidate treasury

📋 Implementation Plan

  1. Transfer DAI – Multisig executes vault transfer of exactly 1 083.2467 DAI to 0x49d3…d30E.

  2. Swap – Wallet performs single Uniswap v3 trade (DAI→POL, slippage ≤ 0.5 %, 10-minute deadline).

  3. Return POL – Immediately forward all POL to Treasury 0xf8b1…8924.

  4. Report – Post all three transaction hashes in Discord’s #treasury-updates and link them in proposal comments.

✅ Rationale

  • Network consistency – Grants and incentives are Polygon-native; holding POL removes FX friction.

  • Gas efficiency – Spending from a single Polygon treasury cuts bridging and swap costs.

  • Strategic upside – Early accumulation of POL provides future staking/governance benefits.

🔒 Safeguards

  • Swap tx capped at 0.5 % slippage; deadline prevents MEV.

  • POL held only in the DAO Treasury; any use or staking requires a new DAO vote.

🗳️ Voting Options

  • YES — Execute the three-step transfer → swap → return detailed above.

  • NO — Retain DAI in the vault and reassess treasury strategy.

{"title": "Constitution of Alkebulan: A Regenerative Network Nation-State", "summary": "The constitution supports a decentralized economy with universal basic income, community funding for public goods, and restorative justice, while promoting education, cultural preservation, and sustainable practices. It is designed to evolve through citizen-driven amendments, aiming to create a just, innovative, and self-governed society for the benefit of humanity and the Earth.", "description": "

Constitution of Alkebulan: A Regenerative Network Nation-State

Preamble
We, the citizens of Alkebulan, united by our commitment to individual sovereignty, collective well-being, and the regeneration of Earth, establish this Constitution as the supreme law of our network nation-state. Guided by the principles of decentralization, transparency, and innovation, we seek to create a society where all individuals are empowered to govern themselves, pursue their aspirations, and contribute to the flourishing of humanity and the planet.

Article I: Sovereignty and Rights of the Individual

Section 1: Inherent Sovereignty
Every individual is inherently sovereign and possesses the inalienable right to self-determination, free expression, and personal autonomy.
The sovereignty of the individual shall be respected, protected, and upheld as the highest principle of governance.

Section 2: Rights and Freedoms
Freedom of Expression: Every citizen has the right to freely express their thoughts, ideas, and beliefs without fear of censorship or retribution.
Privacy and Data Ownership: Citizens have the right to control their personal data and privacy. No entity, public or private, shall collect, store, or use personal data without explicit consent.
Digital Identity: Citizens have the right to a secure and verifiable digital identity, under their exclusive control.
Freedom of Association: Citizens may form, join, and participate in decentralized networks, communities, and organizations.
Economic Autonomy: Citizens have the right to engage in commerce, trade, and financial transactions free from unwarranted interference. Decentralized financial systems shall be promoted to ensure economic freedom.

Section 3: Protection of Rights
The government shall establish mechanisms to protect and enforce the rights of citizens, ensuring that all actions by public or private entities respect individual sovereignty.
Any infringement upon the rights and freedoms enumerated in this Constitution shall be subject to peer-based judicial review and redress.

Article II: Government Structure

Section 1: The President
The President is the senior executive of Alkebulan, entrusted with upholding the Constitution, granting citizenship, and guiding the nation’s regenerative mission.
The President allocates 50% of all transaction fees to strategic initiatives for land regeneration, smart city development, and public good, with full transparency.
The President may propose, veto, or fast-track proposals in the national interest, but is accountable to the Privy Council and the citizenry.
The President’s actions are recorded on-chain and subject to review by the Privy Council and Citizens’ Assembly.

Section 2: The MEGA Party
The MEGA Party is composed of trusted advisors and representatives, appointed by the President and confirmed by citizen vote.
The MEGA Party reviews major policy changes, oversees the President’s discretionary spending, and may propose constitutional amendments or call for a vote of no confidence.
The MEGA Party ensures that the President’s actions align with Alkebulan’s regenerative and human-centric values.

Section 3: The Citizens’ Assembly
The Citizens’ Assembly is the primary decision-making body, where all citizens can propose, debate, and vote on laws, policies, and regulations.
All proposals, discussions, and votes are recorded transparently on a blockchain-based platform.
Each citizen has an equal vote; liquid democracy and delegation are permitted, allowing citizens to delegate voting power to trusted representatives for specific issues.
Major changes, such as constitutional amendments or significant policy shifts, require binding referendums.

Section 4: Delegated Committees and Autonomous Zones
Specialized committees, formed by citizen initiative or Assembly mandate, execute the will of the Citizens’ Assembly in areas such as defense, public services, and environmental policy.
Citizens may form and join autonomous zones within Alkebulan, operating with their own specific rules as long as they respect the core principles of the nation-state.



Section 5: Smart Contracts for Governance
Governance processes—including law enforcement, budget allocation, and contract enforcement—are governed by smart contracts, ensuring automation, transparency, and accountability.
All government actions are recorded on a public ledger, enabling citizens to audit and challenge decisions.

Article III: Economic System and Regenerative Finance

Section 1: Digital Currency and Economic Infrastructure
Alkebulan adopts a decentralized digital currency as its official medium of exchange, ensuring economic autonomy and financial inclusion.
The economic infrastructure is decentralized, granting citizens access to decentralized financial services (DeFi) such as lending, borrowing, and investing.

Section 2: Public Funding and Bonds
Public funding is managed transparently, with citizens having the right to oversee and approve expenditures.
50% of all transaction fees are allocated to community bonds, funding regenerative projects and public goods.
The remaining 50% is allocated to the President’s discretionary fund, to be used for land regeneration, smart city development, and strategic investments, with full transparency.

Section 3: Universal Basic Income
Every citizen receives a Universal Basic Income (UBI) in the national currency, supporting individual sovereignty and reducing economic dependency.

Section 4: Intellectual Property and Innovation
Citizens have the right to own, share, and monetize their intellectual property through decentralized platforms.
The government shall promote and support open innovation, ensuring that new technologies and ideas are freely accessible to all.

Article IV: Justice and Dispute Resolution

Section 1: Decentralized Judicial System
The judicial system is decentralized, with disputes resolved through community-driven arbitration and smart contracts.
Citizens have the right to a fair and impartial hearing, with the option to choose their arbitrators or mediation methods.

Section 2: Restorative Justice
Alkebulan prioritizes restorative justice, focusing on reconciliation, rehabilitation, and the restoration of relationships.
Punitive measures are a last resort, applied only when necessary to protect the rights and safety of citizens.

Article V: Education, Culture, and Public Services

Section 1: Decentralized Education
Citizens have the right to access high-quality, decentralized educational resources and platforms, enabling lifelong learning and personal development.
The government supports open-source educational initiatives and the creation of decentralized knowledge networks.

Section 2: Cultural Preservation and Innovation
Alkebulan promotes and preserves cultural diversity, encouraging the free expression of cultural identities and traditions.
Citizens are encouraged to contribute to the cultural and intellectual development of the nation through decentralized platforms.

Section 3: Public Services and Infrastructure
Public services, including healthcare, security, and welfare, are provided through decentralized networks, ensuring accessibility, efficiency, and citizen control.
The government facilitates the development of digital infrastructure, prioritizing open-source technologies and decentralized applications.

Article VI: Environmental Regeneration and Sustainability

Section 1: Sustainable Practices
Alkebulan adopts and promotes sustainable practices to minimize environmental impact and ensure the well-being of future generations.
Citizens have the right to participate in environmental decision-making and to hold the government accountable for sustainable practices.

Section 2: Climate Action
Alkebulan actively engages in global climate initiatives and encourages decentralized solutions to environmental challenges.
Citizens and communities are encouraged to collaborate on environmental projects through decentralized platforms.


Article VII: Amendments and Evolution

Section 1: Constitutional Amendments
This Constitution may be amended by a supermajority vote of the citizens, conducted through a decentralized voting platform.
Proposed amendments shall be openly debated and reviewed by the citizenry, ensuring transparency and inclusivity in the constitutional evolution.

Section 2: Continuous Evolution
Alkebulan recognizes the need for continuous evolution and adaptation in response to technological advancements and societal changes.
Citizens are encouraged to propose innovations and improvements to the governance model, ensuring that the nation remains at the forefront of progress.

Conclusion
We, the citizens of Alkebulan, pledge to uphold the principles enshrined in this Constitution, to respect the sovereignty and rights of every individual, and to work together in the pursuit of a just, prosperous, and regenerative society. This Constitution shall serve as the foundation of our network nation-state, guiding us toward a future of freedom, innovation, and shared prosperity for humanity and the Earth.

Ratified on 04/24/2025 by the citizens of Alkebulan.
